      <div id="file-0-copying-firmware-files-to-the-device-tree-mediatek-md-readme" class="Box-body readme blob tmp-p-5 tmp-p-xl-6 gist-border-0" style="overflow: auto" tabindex="0" role="region" aria-label="0. Copying firmware files to the Device Tree - Mediatek.md content, created by lopestom on 02:09AM on January 24, 2024.">
    <article class="markdown-body entry-content container-lg" itemprop="text"><div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 class="heading-element" dir="auto">Preface</h1><a id="user-content-preface" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Preface" href="#preface"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><img class="emoji" title=":atom:" alt=":atom:" src="https://github.githubassets.com/images/icons/emoji/atom.png" height="20" width="20" align="absmiddle"> Last updated on 03/05/2024.</p>
<p dir="auto">🌎 initial file on 01/23/2024.</p>
<p dir="auto">Many users, generally users with little knowledge and no experience, asked me which correct files should be placed in the device
tree<sup><a href="#user-content-fn-%C2%B9-d478351e0c6c005bbe3a82b261d75898" id="user-content-fnref-%c2%b9-d478351e0c6c005bbe3a82b261d75898" data-footnote-ref="" aria-describedby="footnote-label">1</a></sup> for Custom Recovery to generally work.</p>
<p dir="auto">But of course most of these users want to know the files that control the encryption, that is, they want to know the correct files so that the encryption/decryption can work.
In this sense, as there is not much information about this on the internet, I decided to put some words and some experience that I have had over a few years with Mediatek devices.</p>
<p dir="auto">Although most of the information has programming, even though I am an Engineer and Teacher, I always wanted the language to understand to be as simple and easy as possible. Even so, you really need to understand some parts of Linux, Android and programming code. Don't read all of this without prior knowledge<sup><a href="#user-content-fn-%C2%B2-d478351e0c6c005bbe3a82b261d75898" id="user-content-fnref-%c2%b2-d478351e0c6c005bbe3a82b261d75898" data-footnote-ref="" aria-describedby="footnote-label">2</a></sup> of the subject.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 class="heading-element" dir="auto">The idea about this Info Guide</h2><a id="user-content-the-idea-about-this-info-guide" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: The idea about this Info Guide" href="#the-idea-about-this-info-guide"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">This "guide" interacts/complements with some important information from other guides:</p>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/3c1f3eaa66248c56e61acf19ddd4b96c">Custom Recovery for MediaTek devices</a></p>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/685cdd9c71476e9daf95ca612066e23f">Fixing Touchscreen - custom recovery</a></p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-important" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg class="octicon octicon-report mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M0 1.75C0 .784.784 0 1.75 0h12.5C15.216 0 16 .784 16 1.75v9.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.25 13H8.06l-2.573 2.573A1.458 1.458 0 0 1 3 14.543V13H1.75A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 0 11.25Zm1.75-.25a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v9.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h2a.75.75 0 0 1 .75.75v2.19l2.72-2.72a.749.749 0 0 1 .53-.22h6.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-9.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25-.25Zm7 2.25v2.5a.75.75 0 0 1-1.5 0v-2.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0ZM9 9a1 1 0 1 1-2 0 1 1 0 0 1 2 0Z"></path></svg>Important</p><p dir="auto">There is no Android version limit to start follow this guide. Understanding how you will have files from Android 4.4 to Android 13 (new information on how to decrypt userdata must be understood and make correct changes depending on each encryption mode), is something individual and each user must know which tool/software to use.</p>
</div>

<p dir="auto">Crypt-Decrypt</p>

<p dir="auto"><a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#file-9-finishing-md">Finishing & Thanks and Notes</a></p>
<section data-footnotes="" class="footnotes"><h2 id="footnote-label" class="sr-only" dir="auto">Footnotes</h2>
<ol dir="auto">
<li id="user-content-fn-%C2%B9-d478351e0c6c005bbe3a82b261d75898">
<p dir="auto">Device Tree using a Minimal Manifest repository is an informative data structure used to describe the hardware parts of the devices in a platform-independent manner. It provides a way to describe hardware info and or firmware version (if want) of the device without the need for board-specific source code. The objective is compile a Custom Recovery with special options like backup, instalation and modify files. <a href="#user-content-fnref-%C2%B9-d478351e0c6c005bbe3a82b261d75898" data-footnote-backref="" aria-label="Back to reference 1" class="data-footnote-backref">↩</a></p>
</li>
<li id="user-content-fn-%C2%B2-d478351e0c6c005bbe3a82b261d75898">
<p dir="auto">Many times in the past I have encountered people who are truly knowledgeable in creating Custom Recovery. But most of them kept the information to themselves. I cannot judge such a choice. It's quite possible that I didn't intend to save my knowledge until I became a worthless carcass, but I also didn't pass on all the knowledge I have when I helped some people. Well, learn that I don't have ALL the knowledge about Custom Recovery and I'm not going to write everything I know either. Most of what you learn from this is definitely reading and doing, making mistakes and learning to correct until you find the perfect skill for you. <a href="#user-content-fnref-%C2%B2-d478351e0c6c005bbe3a82b261d75898" data-footnote-backref="" aria-label="Back to reference 2" class="data-footnote-backref">↩</a></p>
</li>
</ol>
</section>

    <article class="markdown-body entry-content container-lg" itemprop="text"><div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 class="heading-element" dir="auto">1. What you need?</h2><a id="user-content-1-what-you-need" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1. What you need?" href="#1-what-you-need"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 class="heading-element" dir="auto">1.1 Tools/Software</h3><a id="user-content-11-toolssoftware" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1.1 Tools/Software" href="#11-toolssoftware"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Depending on what you know and what you want, tools/software and knowledge are important. Knowing whether the files are in the EROFS, sparse or ext4 extension is relatively easy. So understand that the tool must have the ability to work with the file extensions you will need.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 class="heading-element" dir="auto">1.1.1 Firmware img files</h4><a id="user-content-111-firmware-img-files" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1.1.1 Firmware img files" href="#111-firmware-img-files"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">There are many tools/software in different ways for Linux, Win8.1~11 and even for Android (need ROOT).
The particularity at this point is not to highlight how these tools/software are used. But you can have a direction for them:</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th align="center">Linux</th>
<th align="center">WinNN</th>
<th align="center">Android</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td align="center"><a href="https://srkfree.sr-code.com/" rel="nofollow">SuperRs-Kitchen</a></td>
<td align="center"><a href="https://www.crbplus.com/" rel="nofollow">CRB Android Kitchen</a></td>
<td align="center"><a href="https://github.com/blackeangel/UKA">UKA</a></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<p dir="auto">These tools are for unpacking files like super.img and/or vendor_dlkm.img. Some can unpack files like recovery.img, boot.img and vendor_boot.img.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 class="heading-element" dir="auto">1.1.2 Recovery ramdisk img files</h4><a id="user-content-112-recovery-ramdisk-img-files" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1.1.2 Recovery ramdisk img files" href="#112-recovery-ramdisk-img-files"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">To unpack files like recovery.img, boot.img and vendor_boot.img in particular and save time to have files and information, we can use the most updated tool that exists for WinNN and Linux. Generally the tools/software highlighted above also use the same tool/software as this one: <a href="https://github.com/osm0sis/Android-Image-Kitchen/tree/AIK-Linux">AIK Linux</a> -- <a href="https://github.com/osm0sis/Android-Image-Kitchen/tree/master">AIK WinNN</a> -- <a href="https://github.com/osm0sis/Android-Image-Kitchen/tree/AIK-mobile">AIK-mobile</a></p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 class="heading-element" dir="auto">1.1.3 Editing text files</h4><a id="user-content-113-editing-text-files" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1.1.3 Editing text files" href="#113-editing-text-files"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">There are many of them. But I can point out some that do something better and really highlight usability.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">WinNN</p>
<p dir="auto">To edit text files like <code>*.rc, *.prop, *.default, *.fstab, fstab.*</code> and other files that have no extension but can be opened through the text editor, you can use the tool /software <a href="https://notepad-plus-plus.org/downloads/" rel="nofollow">Notepad++</a>.
To compare text files, you can use the tool /software <a href="https://www.scootersoftware.com/" rel="nofollow">Beyond Compare</a>.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-tip" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg class="octicon octicon-light-bulb mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M8 1.5c-2.363 0-4 1.69-4 3.75 0 .984.424 1.625.984 2.304l.214.253c.223.264.47.556.673.848.284.411.537.896.621 1.49a.75.75 0 0 1-1.484.211c-.04-.282-.163-.547-.37-.847a8.456 8.456 0 0 0-.542-.68c-.084-.1-.173-.205-.268-.32C3.201 7.75 2.5 6.766 2.5 5.25 2.5 2.31 4.863 0 8 0s5.5 2.31 5.5 5.25c0 1.516-.701 2.5-1.328 3.259-.095.115-.184.22-.268.319-.207.245-.383.453-.541.681-.208.3-.33.565-.37.847a.751.751 0 0 1-1.485-.212c.084-.593.337-1.078.621-1.489.203-.292.45-.584.673-.848.075-.088.147-.173.213-.253.561-.679.985-1.32.985-2.304 0-2.06-1.637-3.75-4-3.75ZM5.75 12h4.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-4.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0-1.5ZM6 15.25a.75.75 0 0 1 .75-.75h2.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-2.5a.75.75 0 0 1-.75-.75Z"></path></svg>Tip</p><p dir="auto">Depending on what you want to use, these tools/software can start on Linux with Wine.</p>
</div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">Linux</p>
<p dir="auto">To edit text files like <code>*.rc, *.prop, *.default, *.fstab, fstab.*</code> and other files that have no extension but can be opened through the text editor, you can use the tool /software <a href="https://www.vim.org/download.php" rel="nofollow">Vi/Vim</a> or <a href="https://kate-editor.org/" rel="nofollow">kate</a>.
To compare text files, you can use the tool /software <a href="https://meldmerge.org/" rel="nofollow">Meld</a> or <a href="https://www.diffchecker.com/" rel="nofollow">Diffchecker</a>.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-tip" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg class="octicon octicon-light-bulb mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M8 1.5c-2.363 0-4 1.69-4 3.75 0 .984.424 1.625.984 2.304l.214.253c.223.264.47.556.673.848.284.411.537.896.621 1.49a.75.75 0 0 1-1.484.211c-.04-.282-.163-.547-.37-.847a8.456 8.456 0 0 0-.542-.68c-.084-.1-.173-.205-.268-.32C3.201 7.75 2.5 6.766 2.5 5.25 2.5 2.31 4.863 0 8 0s5.5 2.31 5.5 5.25c0 1.516-.701 2.5-1.328 3.259-.095.115-.184.22-.268.319-.207.245-.383.453-.541.681-.208.3-.33.565-.37.847a.751.751 0 0 1-1.485-.212c.084-.593.337-1.078.621-1.489.203-.292.45-.584.673-.848.075-.088.147-.173.213-.253.561-.679.985-1.32.985-2.304 0-2.06-1.637-3.75-4-3.75ZM5.75 12h4.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-4.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0-1.5ZM6 15.25a.75.75 0 0 1 .75-.75h2.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-2.5a.75.75 0 0 1-.75-.75Z"></path></svg>Tip</p><p dir="auto">Depending on what you want to use, these tools/software can start in the WinNN too.</p>
</div>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-note" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg class="octicon octicon-info mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M0 8a8 8 0 1 1 16 0A8 8 0 0 1 0 8Zm8-6.5a6.5 6.5 0 1 0 0 13 6.5 6.5 0 0 0 0-13ZM6.5 7.75A.75.75 0 0 1 7.25 7h1a.75.75 0 0 1 .75.75v2.75h.25a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-2a.75.75 0 0 1 0-1.5h.25v-2h-.25a.75.75 0 0 1-.75-.75ZM8 6a1 1 0 1 1 0-2 1 1 0 0 1 0 2Z"></path></svg>Note</p><p dir="auto">These are just as references. Use the tool/software that you know is best and works.</p>
</div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 class="heading-element" dir="auto">1.2 Unpacking img files</h3><a id="user-content-12-unpacking-img-files" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1.2 Unpacking img files" href="#12-unpacking-img-files"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">We start by understanding that you already know how to unpack the img files from the device's firmware.
So you must have enough space and time to wait for the process. In this sense, you will have the files as a basis for this piece of information: <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/ce250f5de64a2764ee85092a2c01939e">Android - Some information about firmware files</a></p>
<p dir="auto">The main files to unpack are:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>super.img</code> 🔛 this generates the <code>system.img</code> files; <code>vendor.img</code>; <code>product.img</code> which must be unpacked as well,
Depending on the OEM, some extra files may be contained in super.img as partitions of <code>system_ext.img</code>; <code>my_preload.img</code>; <code>mi_ext.img</code>; among other img files;</li>
<li><code>recovery.img</code> or <code>boot.img</code> or <code>vendor_boot.img</code> 🔛 thi's a base the files for recovery_ramdisk;</li>
<li><code>vendor_dlkm.img</code> 🔛 starting from A13+, here we have <code>*.ko</code> files which are the hardware driver modules.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 class="heading-element" dir="auto">1.3 Which img file is best to start with?</h3><a id="user-content-13-which-img-file-is-best-to-start-with" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1.3 Which img file is best to start with?" href="#13-which-img-file-is-best-to-start-with"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">By objective and experience, the best file will be the one with the ramdisk part. Therefore, depending on the kernel version, the recovery ramdisk may be inside the <code>recovery.img</code> or <code>boot.img</code> or <code>vendor_boot.img</code> file.
For Android with kernel version 4.nn and earlier, you must use recovery.img and boot.img files as recovery ramdisk. This also depends on the Android version and to know what to use, just look at the firmware files which img file exists in it.
For Android with kernel version 5.nn and later, you must use the vendor_boot.img file as recovery ramdisk.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2. The recovery ramdisk</h2><a id="user-content-2-the-recovery-ramdisk" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2. The recovery ramdisk" href="#2-the-recovery-ramdisk"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">We start with the recovery.img or boot.img or vendor_boot.img files to understand the information that is written in **.so files and text files.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2.1 Parts of recovery ramdisk</h3><a id="user-content-21-parts-of-recovery-ramdisk" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2.1 Parts of recovery ramdisk" href="#21-parts-of-recovery-ramdisk"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">When you unpack the img file, there are generally two parts/folders:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">Split or files_info => Structure and informations by kernel files;</p>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/25868008/299470688-e6ca9a27-9346-4d04-bf0f-1364797e65b8.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..5QXTPWPTyTDvu8uQjhdVnVPiFAH1XdLgXIVzpURsIbA"><img src="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/25868008/299470688-e6ca9a27-9346-4d04-bf0f-1364797e65b8.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..5QXTPWPTyTDvu8uQjhdVnVPiFAH1XdLgXIVzpURsIbA" alt="ksnip_20240124-174502" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">Ramdisk or cpio => Folder structure and informations with very different files;</p>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2.2 What informations does Device Tree need?</h3><a id="user-content-22-what-informations-does-device-tree-need" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2.2 What informations does Device Tree need?" href="#22-what-informations-does-device-tree-need"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">I highlighted in the image above some information that will be necessary to place in the Device Tree.
Then consider the Split or files_info information for the Device Tree 1️⃣<code>BoardConfig.mk</code>; 2️⃣<code>device.mk</code></p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2.2.1 Split or files_info</h4><a id="user-content-221-split-or-files_info" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2.2.1 Split or files_info" href="#221-split-or-files_info"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<p dir="auto">You can have this information on the unpacking screen or just click on each file to open and see the information given.
For better visualization and understanding:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">Supplied image: recovery-MT6761.img</p>
<p dir="auto">It is important to understand and know the correct recovery ramdisk partition. This gives us information about what exists in a given img file with its structure and files. Furthermore, install the Custom recovery img file on the correct partition. Understand that I already wrote about this and there are three partitions where the recovery ramdisk part can remain.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>

<p dir="auto">In summary, the information will look like this in BoardConfig.mk</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Kernel</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_KERNEL_CMDLINE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">bootopt</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">64</span><span class="pl-c1">S3</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-c1">32</span><span class="pl-c1">N2</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-c1">64</span><span class="pl-c1">N2</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_KERNEL_BASE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">0x40078000</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_KERNEL_PAGESIZE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">2048</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_HASH_TYPE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">sha1</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_KERNEL_OFFSET</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">0x00008000</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_RAMDISK_OFFSET</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">0x11a88000</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_KERNEL_SECOND_OFFSET</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">0xbff88000</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_KERNEL_TAGS_OFFSET</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">0x07808000</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_BOOTIMG_HEADER_VERSION</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">2</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_DTB_OFFSET</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">0x07808000</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS</span> += --<span class="pl-en">base</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">BOARD_KERNEL_BASE</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS</span> += --<span class="pl-en">pagesize</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">BOARD_KERNEL_PAGESIZE</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS</span> += --<span class="pl-en">ramdisk_offset</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">BOARD_RAMDISK_OFFSET</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS</span> += --<span class="pl-en">tags_offset</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">BOARD_KERNEL_TAGS_OFFSET</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS</span> += --<span class="pl-en">kernel_offset</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">BOARD_KERNEL_OFFSET</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS</span> += --<span class="pl-en">second_offset</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">BOARD_KERNEL_SECOND_OFFSET</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS</span> += --<span class="pl-en">dtb_offset</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">BOARD_DTB_OFFSET</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS</span> += --<span class="pl-en">header_version</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">BOARD_BOOTIMG_HEADER_VERSION</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS</span> += --<span class="pl-en">dtb</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">TARGET_PREBUILT_DTB</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>

<span class="pl-c"># Hack: prevent anti rollback</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PLATFORM_SECURITY_PATCH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">2022</span>-<span class="pl-c1">02</span>-<span class="pl-c1">05</span>
<span class="pl-c1">VENDOR_SECURITY_PATCH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">2022</span>-<span class="pl-c1">02</span>-<span class="pl-c1">05</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PLATFORM_VERSION</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">11</span></pre></div>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-note" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg class="octicon octicon-info mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M0 8a8 8 0 1 1 16 0A8 8 0 0 1 0 8Zm8-6.5a6.5 6.5 0 1 0 0 13 6.5 6.5 0 0 0 0-13ZM6.5 7.75A.75.75 0 0 1 7.25 7h1a.75.75 0 0 1 .75.75v2.75h.25a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-2a.75.75 0 0 1 0-1.5h.25v-2h-.25a.75.75 0 0 1-.75-.75ZM8 6a1 1 0 1 1 0-2 1 1 0 0 1 0 2Z"></path></svg>Note</p><p dir="auto">Each part of <code>BOARD_KERNEL_*</code> needs <code>BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS</code> for the source code to create a file with the size value property defined.
For 32-bits devices the <code>BOARD_KERNEL_CMDLINE</code> has	<code>bootopt=64S3,32S1,32S1</code>.</p>
</div>

<span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_SHIPPING_API_LEVEL</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">30</span>

<p dir="auto">Some SDK/API informations: <a href="https://apilevels.com/" rel="nofollow">Android API Levels</a></p>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-caution" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg class="octicon octicon-stop mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4.47.22A.749.749 0 0 1 5 0h6c.199 0 .389.079.53.22l4.25 4.25c.141.14.22.331.22.53v6a.749.749 0 0 1-.22.53l-4.25 4.25A.749.749 0 0 1 11 16H5a.749.749 0 0 1-.53-.22L.22 11.53A.749.749 0 0 1 0 11V5c0-.199.079-.389.22-.53Zm.84 1.28L1.5 5.31v5.38l3.81 3.81h5.38l3.81-3.81V5.31L10.69 1.5ZM8 4a.75.75 0 0 1 .75.75v3.5a.75.75 0 0 1-1.5 0v-3.5A.75.75 0 0 1 8 4Zm0 8a1 1 0 1 1 0-2 1 1 0 0 1 0 2Z"></path></svg>Caution</p><p dir="auto">Some information may be empty like <code>BOARD_NAME</code>. It usually appears on devices from the following brands: Realme; Infinix; Tecno; Cubot; Sansumg.
Other information such as <code>BOARD_RECOVERY_DTBO_OFFSET</code>; <code>BOARD_RECOVERY_DTBO_SIZE</code> is not priority/necessary.
Only include information that is relevant and can be considered absolutely safe/correct. Therefore, do not take risks due to excessive doubts. This may cause errors in compiling the Custom Recovery img file.</p>
</div>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-tip" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg class="octicon octicon-light-bulb mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M8 1.5c-2.363 0-4 1.69-4 3.75 0 .984.424 1.625.984 2.304l.214.253c.223.264.47.556.673.848.284.411.537.896.621 1.49a.75.75 0 0 1-1.484.211c-.04-.282-.163-.547-.37-.847a8.456 8.456 0 0 0-.542-.68c-.084-.1-.173-.205-.268-.32C3.201 7.75 2.5 6.766 2.5 5.25 2.5 2.31 4.863 0 8 0s5.5 2.31 5.5 5.25c0 1.516-.701 2.5-1.328 3.259-.095.115-.184.22-.268.319-.207.245-.383.453-.541.681-.208.3-.33.565-.37.847a.751.751 0 0 1-1.485-.212c.084-.593.337-1.078.621-1.489.203-.292.45-.584.673-.848.075-.088.147-.173.213-.253.561-.679.985-1.32.985-2.304 0-2.06-1.637-3.75-4-3.75ZM5.75 12h4.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-4.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0-1.5ZM6 15.25a.75.75 0 0 1 .75-.75h2.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-2.5a.75.75 0 0 1-.75-.75Z"></path></svg>Tip</p><p dir="auto">If your device tree need <code>BOARD_NAME</code> so you need <code>BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS += --board $(BOARD_NAME)</code></p>

      <div id="file-2-mediatek-devices-vs-knowledge-md-readme" class="Box-body readme blob tmp-p-5 tmp-p-xl-6 gist-border-0" style="overflow: auto" tabindex="0" role="region" aria-label="2. Mediatek devices vs knowledge.md content, created by lopestom on 02:09AM on January 24, 2024.">
    <article class="markdown-body entry-content container-lg" itemprop="text"><div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-important" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg class="octicon octicon-report mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M0 1.75C0 .784.784 0 1.75 0h12.5C15.216 0 16 .784 16 1.75v9.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.25 13H8.06l-2.573 2.573A1.458 1.458 0 0 1 3 14.543V13H1.75A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 0 11.25Zm1.75-.25a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v9.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h2a.75.75 0 0 1 .75.75v2.19l2.72-2.72a.749.749 0 0 1 .53-.22h6.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-9.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25-.25Zm7 2.25v2.5a.75.75 0 0 1-1.5 0v-2.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0ZM9 9a1 1 0 1 1-2 0 1 1 0 0 1 2 0Z"></path></svg>Important</p><p dir="auto">Before approaching the following continuation, it is important to highlight that some basic knowledge is necessary.
Understanding that we must know what the device has in terms of hardware and software information will be important to avoid having doubts or confusing information.
In this context, follow this step to obtain all possible information: <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/3c1f3eaa66248c56e61acf19ddd4b96c#ii-get-some-informations-from-device-using-adb-fastboot-platform-tool">II. Get some informations from device using adb-fastboot platform-tool</a></p>
</div>
<p dir="auto">As the main approach is for Mediatek devices, even these have some different peculiarities. Some information should initially be understood as:</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th align="left">Platform</th>
<th align="center">Display</th>
<th align="center">Memory</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td align="left">Chipset</td>
<td align="center">Resolution</td>
<td align="center">Internal</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td align="left">CPU</td>
<td align="center">Type</td>
<td align="center"></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td align="left">GPU</td>
<td align="center">Camera cutout</td>
<td align="center">Type</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<p dir="auto">In this matter, many Mediatek chipsets exist and are also built with 32bits or 64bits; with Quadcore or Octacore and finally the Kernel Version and the Android OS Version.
Some Mediatek chipsets/Processor Name to know: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_MediaTek_systems_on_chips" rel="nofollow">Model number</a> - <a href="https://deviceinfohw.ru/devices/index.php?platform=PLATFORM&cpu=CPU&brand=BRAND&filter_key=KEY&filter=mt&submit=Search" rel="nofollow">PLATFORM</a> - <a href="https://helpix-ru.translate.goog/chipset/?_x_tr_sl=auto&_x_tr_tl=en&_x_tr_hl=pt-BR&_x_tr_pto=wapp" rel="nofollow">Chipsets MediaTek</a></p>
<p dir="auto">As there are three types of partitions that can contain the recovery ramdisk - <code>recovery.img</code> or <code>boot.img</code> or <code>vendor_boot.img</code> file - there are also particularities when entering configuration information about these partitions. This part must be understood so that we do not include extra and wrong information.</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th align="left">Partition</th>
<th align="center">dependency</th>
<th align="center">kernel</th>
<th align="center">dtb</th>
<th align="center">dtbo</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td align="left">recovery</td>
<td align="center">boot.img</td>
<td align="center">yes</td>
<td align="center">yes</td>
<td align="center">yes</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td align="left">boot</td>
<td align="center">first_stage_ramdisk folder</td>
<td align="center">yes</td>
<td align="center">yes</td>
<td align="center">no</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td align="left">vendor_boot</td>
<td align="center">first_stage_ramdisk folder</td>
<td align="center">no</td>
<td align="center">yes</td>
<td align="center">no</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<p dir="auto">How do we know if such files are on the partition?
See the unpacked parts of <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#21-parts-of-recovery-ramdisk">Split or files_info</a>.</p>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-caution" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg class="octicon octicon-stop mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4.47.22A.749.749 0 0 1 5 0h6c.199 0 .389.079.53.22l4.25 4.25c.141.14.22.331.22.53v6a.749.749 0 0 1-.22.53l-4.25 4.25A.749.749 0 0 1 11 16H5a.749.749 0 0 1-.53-.22L.22 11.53A.749.749 0 0 1 0 11V5c0-.199.079-.389.22-.53Zm.84 1.28L1.5 5.31v5.38l3.81 3.81h5.38l3.81-3.81V5.31L10.69 1.5ZM8 4a.75.75 0 0 1 .75.75v3.5a.75.75 0 0 1-1.5 0v-3.5A.75.75 0 0 1 8 4Zm0 8a1 1 0 1 1 0-2 1 1 0 0 1 0 2Z"></path></svg>Caution</p><p dir="auto">As a general rule, I will just interact between some basic settings and flags, indicating which file the information should come from. So keep in mind that Device Tree files only serve as a way of learning where information is and where to put it. The Device Tree files below are not intended to serve as an example or copy for a given Chipset. There are many repositories on github that may be good for your device/Chipset and therefore you can use them.</p>
</div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2.2.2 Ramdisk or cpio</h4><a id="user-content-222-ramdisk-or-cpio" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2.2.2 Ramdisk or cpio" href="#222-ramdisk-or-cpio"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/25868008/299479168-bec7da6a-b97e-4943-877d-be56d2501925.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..bi8225OHIvPoBW-QRZd7gGwCZtfWCcwq20_uqxYX4-0"><img src="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/25868008/299479168-bec7da6a-b97e-4943-877d-be56d2501925.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..bi8225OHIvPoBW-QRZd7gGwCZtfWCcwq20_uqxYX4-0" alt="ksnip_20240124-174704" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<p dir="auto">I highlighted in the image above some information that will be necessary to place in the Device Tree.
Then consider the Recovery Ramdisk information for the Device Tree 1️⃣<code>BoardConfig.mk</code>; 2️⃣<code>device.mk</code>; 3️⃣<code>twrp_devicename.mk</code>; 4️⃣<code>Android.mk</code>; 5️⃣<code>AndroidProducts.mk</code></p>
<p dir="auto">The important information and files from the Stock img file will be in ramdisk\ prop.default & init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc; ramdisk\first_stage_ramdisk\fstab.mtNNNN & fstab.emmc ; ramdisk\lib\modules/*.ko; ramdisk\system\bin\mtk_plpath_utils; ramdisk\system\lib64\hw\android.hardware.boot@'N.n-impl-N.n-mtkimpl.so and ramdisk\system\etc\recovery.fstab</p>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-warning" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg class="octicon octicon-alert mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M6.457 1.047c.659-1.234 2.427-1.234 3.086 0l6.082 11.378A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.082 15H1.918a1.75 1.75 0 0 1-1.543-2.575Zm1.763.707a.25.25 0 0 0-.44 0L1.698 13.132a.25.25 0 0 0 .22.368h12.164a.25.25 0 0 0 .22-.368Zm.53 3.996v2.5a.75.75 0 0 1-1.5 0v-2.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0ZM9 11a1 1 0 1 1-2 0 1 1 0 0 1 2 0Z"></path></svg>Warning</p><p dir="auto">Settings are understood as information given from Android  source code and flags coming from the TWRP source code that are highlighted with <code>TW_</code> and that can help with Custom Recovery processes and features.
There are many settings and flags that depend on what the device can have. You can read more in the InfoGuide <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/3c1f3eaa66248c56e61acf19ddd4b96c#now-before-you-publish-the-device-tree-in-the-github-repository-so-maybe-you-want-understand-about-some-flags-setup--configurations-that-teamwin-source-code-have-after-that-so-you-can-understand-what-we-have-in-the-androidmk-androidproductsmk-devicemk-twrp_devicemk-vendorsetupsh-primarily-in-the-boardconfigmk">understand about some flags - setup & configurations</a>. You can compare <code>TW_</code> flags for each Custom Recovery version if you want need know more so look <a href="https://github.com/TeamWin/android_bootable_recovery/blob/android-10.0/variables.h">TWRP-10</a>; <a href="https://github.com/TeamWin/android_bootable_recovery/blob/android-11/variables.h">TWRP-11</a>; <a href="https://github.com/TeamWin/android_bootable_recovery/blob/android-12.1/variables.h">TWRP-12.1</a>.</p>
<p dir="auto">Be careful when using any flag from a certain TWRP version to another TWRP version. As well as the Android version Variable /Settings for another Android version from the AOSP base. Each version of TWRP may have new flags introduced and may also have Deprecated flags.</p>
<p dir="auto">Therefore, the approach below only serves as an information base for a given device, but obviously it can have many characteristics that are the same or different from other devices.</p>
</div>
</article>

      <div id="file-3-boardconfig-mk_-md-readme" class="Box-body readme blob tmp-p-5 tmp-p-xl-6 gist-border-0" style="overflow: auto" tabindex="0" role="region" aria-label="3. BoardConfig.mk_.md content, created by lopestom on 02:09AM on January 24, 2024.">
    <article class="markdown-body entry-content container-lg" itemprop="text"><div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h5 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2.2.2.1 The *.mk files</h5><a id="user-content-2221-the-mk-files" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2.2.2.1 The *.mk files" href="#2221-the-mk-files"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<pre><code>part I of III
</code></pre>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h5 class="heading-element" dir="auto">1️⃣ BoardConfig.mk</h5><a id="user-content-one-boardconfigmk" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: :one: BoardConfig.mk" href="#one-boardconfigmk"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c1">DEVICE_PATH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">device</span>/<span class="pl-en">brand</span>/<span class="pl-en">devicename</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">brand ➡️ from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by any <code>*.brand=</code> line</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">devicename ➡️ from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by any <code>*.device=</code> line</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># For building with minimal manifest</span>
<span class="pl-c1">ALLOW_MISSING_DEPENDENCIES</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">Indicates that the source code of Custom Recovery based on <strong>minimal manifest</strong> programming and will use the <strong>kernel file</strong> copied in the prebuilt folder.</p>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre>\# <span class="pl-v">Architecture</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_ARCH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">arm64</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_ARCH_VARIANT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">armv8</span>-<span class="pl-en">a</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_CPU_ABI</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">arm64</span>-<span class="pl-en">v8a</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_CPU_ABI2</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_CPU_VARIANT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">cortex</span>-<span class="pl-en">a53</span>

<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_2ND_ARCH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">arm</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_2ND_ARCH_VARIANT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">armv8</span>-<span class="pl-en">a</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_2ND_CPU_ABI</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">armeabi</span>-<span class="pl-en">v7a</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_2ND_CPU_ABI2</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">armeabi</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_2ND_CPU_VARIANT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">cortex</span>-<span class="pl-en">a53</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">Since I wrote another InfoGuide <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/3c1f3eaa66248c56e61acf19ddd4b96c#build-levels">Custom Recovery for MediaTek devices</a>, I don't want to repeat too much about this or go into detail. Writing about the Android structure is not the focus at the moment and without a minimum knowledge of Linux-Android, it becomes more difficult to understand it all.
<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ARM_architecture_family" rel="nofollow">ARM architecture family</a> - <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ARM_processors" rel="nofollow">List of ARM processors</a> - <a href="https://community.arm.com/arm-community-blogs/b/architectures-and-processors-blog/posts/armv8-a-architecture-evolution" rel="nofollow">Armv8-A architecture evolution</a></p>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://developer.android.com/ndk/guides/abis#sa" rel="nofollow">Architecture</a> 🔀 But for the sake of knowledge, just understand that there are devices with 32-bits (arm) and 64-bits (arm64).</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">TARGET_ARCH ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by any <code>ro.bionic.arch=</code> or/and <code>ro.bionic.2nd_arch=</code> line.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TARGET_ARCH_VARIANT ➡️ The <em>Variant</em> comes from the Architecture and depends on each device. Usually <em><code>armv8-a</code></em> or <em><code>armv8-2a</code></em> for <strong>64-bits</strong> devices & <em><code>armeabi-v7a</code></em> for <strong>32-bits</strong> devices. More details, check the links I left above.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TARGET_CPU_ABI ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by any <code>ro.product.cpu.abi=</code> or/and <code>ro.product.cpu.abilist64=</code> or/and <code>ro.product.cpu.abilist=</code> or/and <code>ro.product.cpu.abilist32=</code> line.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TARGET_CPU_ABI2 ➡️ Usually with nothing.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://developer.android.com/ndk/guides/android_mk#tiv" rel="nofollow">Target information variables</a> 🔀 This will indicate which processor will be in the device with generic efficiency. The <em><a href="https://developer.android.com/ndk/guides/cpu-features" rel="nofollow">CPU</a></em> will determine this part.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">TARGET_CPU_VARIANT ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by any <code>ro.bionic.cpu_variant=</code> or/and <code>ro.bionic.2nd_cpu_variant=</code> or/and <code>dalvik.vm.isa.arm64.variant=</code> or/and <code>ro.bionic.2nd_arch=</code> or/and <code>dalvik.vm.isa.arm.variant=</code> line.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TARGET_2ND ➡️ For devices with 64-bits & 32-bits.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">If you have only 32-bits device:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Architecture</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_ARCH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">arm</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_ARCH_VARIANT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">armv7</span>-<span class="pl-en">a</span>-<span class="pl-en">neon</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_CPU_ABI</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">armeabi</span>-<span class="pl-en">v7a</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_CPU_ABI2</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">armeabi</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_CPU_VARIANT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">generic</span>

<span class="pl-c"># Architecture Extensions</span>
<span class="pl-c1">ARCH_ARM_HAVE_NEON</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_CPU_SMP</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">false</span>
<span class="pl-c1">ARCH_ARM_HAVE_TLS_REGISTER</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span></pre></div>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c1">TARGET_BOARD_SUFFIX</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">_64</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_USES_64_BIT_BINDER</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_SUPPORTS_64_BIT_APPS</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_IS_64_BIT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>TARGET_BOARD_SUFFIX ➡️ Set for 64-bit devices.</li>
<li>TARGET_USES_64_BIT_BINDER ➡️ Should be set even while doing a 32-bit only build for a 64-bit arch. <strong><code>Maybe it's deprecated</code></strong>.</li>
<li>TARGET_SUPPORTS_64_BIT_APPS ➡️ Set for 64-bit devices.</li>
<li>TARGET_IS_64_BIT ➡️ Set for 64-bit devices.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">If you have only 32-bits device:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c1">ARCH_ARM_HAVE_NEON</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_CPU_SMP</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">false</span>
<span class="pl-c1">ARCH_ARM_HAVE_TLS_REGISTER</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>

<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_BOARD_SUFFIX</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">_32</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_SUPPORTS_32_BIT_APPS</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_SUPPORTS_64_BIT_APPS</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">false</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_IS_32_BIT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span></pre></div>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Platform</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_BOARD_PLATFORM</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">mt6765</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_BOARD_PLATFORM_GPU</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">mtk</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>TARGET_BOARD_PLATFORM ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by any <code>ro.board.platform=</code>.</li>
<li>TARGET_BOARD_PLATFORM_GPU ➡️ It comes from the <code>GPU specification</code>. Generally you should look for this part. Example:
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre>  <span class="pl-c"># Platform</span>
  <span class="pl-c1">TARGET_BOARD_PLATFORM_GPU</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">mali</span>-<span class="pl-en">g76mc4</span></pre></div>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Bootloader</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_BOOTLOADER_BOARD_NAME</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">k62v1_64_h5</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_NO_BOOTLOADER</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_USES_UEFI</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>TARGET_BOOTLOADER_BOARD_NAME ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by any <code>ro.product.board=</code>.</li>
<li>TARGET_NO_BOOTLOADER ➡️ <code>true</code> means that we're not building the bootloader...</li>
<li>TARGET_USES_UEFI ➡️ If it's set to "true" or any non-empty value, it means that the target platform supports UEFI and the software should be designed and built with UEFI support. If it is set to "false" or an empty value, it means that the target platform does not use UEFI and the software can be built without UEFI support. You can find out if this exists if you get information with logcat, getprop, dmesg on the device. If in doubt, delete TARGET_USES_UEFI of this part.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># MTK Hardware</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_HAS_MTK_HARDWARE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_USES_MTK_HARDWARE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">MTK_HARDWARE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Do I need to explain this part?</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Assert</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_OTA_ASSERT_DEVICE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">H166</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by any <code>*.device=</code> and/or <code>*.model=</code>.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Example:
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre>    <span class="pl-c"># Assert</span>
    <span class="pl-c1">TARGET_OTA_ASSERT_DEVICE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">HCE600</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-c1">SOYES</span> <span class="pl-v">MAXo</span></pre></div>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># File systems and partitions</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_HAS_LARGE_FILESYSTEM</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_BOOTIMAGE_PARTITION_SIZE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">0x2000000</span> <span class="pl-c"># 33554432 - 32MB</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_RECOVERYIMAGE_PARTITION_SIZE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">0x2000000</span> <span class="pl-c"># 33554432 - 32MB</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_CACHEIMAGE_FILE_SYSTEM_TYPE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">ext4</span>
<span class="pl-c">#BOARD_CACHEIMAGE_PARTITION_SIZE := 0x1B000000</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_SYSTEMIMAGE_FILE_SYSTEM_TYPE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">ext4</span>
<span class="pl-c">#BOARD_SYSTEMIMAGE_PARTITION_SIZE := 0x4A939000</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_USERDATAIMAGE_FILE_SYSTEM_TYPE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">ext4</span>
<span class="pl-c">#BOARD_USERDATAIMAGE_PARTITION_SIZE := 0xD48CF8000</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_VENDORIMAGE_FILE_SYSTEM_TYPE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">ext4</span>
<span class="pl-c">#BOARD_VENDORIMAGE_PARTITION_SIZE := 0x1FCD3000</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">The information in this part comes from <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#21-parts-of-recovery-ramdisk">Split</a> or <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#221-split-or-files_info">files_info</a>.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>BOARD_HAS_LARGE_FILESYSTEM ➡️ Use this flag if the board has a partition larger than 2GB.</li>
<li>BOARD_BOOTIMAGE_PARTITION_SIZE ➡️ The real size of boot.img file. You can put Hexadecimal or Decimal value.</li>
<li>BOARD_RECOVERYIMAGE_PARTITION_SIZE ➡️ The real size of recovery.img file. You can put Hexadecimal or Decimal value.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">*** See the figure below [<code>fastboot getvar all</code> example] which allows you to have the correct sizes.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">BOARD_CACHEIMAGE_FILE_SYSTEM_TYPE & BOARD_SYSTEMIMAGE_FILE_SYSTEM_TYPE & BOARD_USERDATAIMAGE_FILE_SYSTEM_TYPE & BOARD_VENDORIMAGE_FILE_SYSTEM_TYPE ➡️ Comes from stock <strong>/ramdisk/system/etc/<code>recovery.fstab</code></strong> file by fstype.</p>
<details><summary>Click to open recovery.fstab example</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://gist.github.com/assets/25868008/45f9ec7c-2570-4a55-b796-3ab7ebbd28bd"><img src="https://gist.github.com/assets/25868008/45f9ec7c-2570-4a55-b796-3ab7ebbd28bd" alt="ksnip_20240126-201557" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">BOARD_CACHEIMAGE_PARTITION_SIZE & BOARD_SYSTEMIMAGE_PARTITION_SIZE & BOARD_USERDATAIMAGE_PARTITION_SIZE & BOARD_VENDORIMAGE_PARTITION_SIZE ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>fastboot</em></strong> and <strong><em>fastbootd</em></strong> comand  with <code>fastboot getvar all</code>.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Dynamic Partition</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_SUPER_PARTITION_SIZE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">6442450944</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_SUPER_PARTITION_GROUPS</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">main</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_MAIN_SIZE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">6438256640</span> <span class="pl-c"># (BOARD_SUPER_PARTITION_SIZE - 4MB)</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_MAIN_PARTITION_LIST</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">product</span> <span class="pl-en">vendor</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">odm</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">BOARD_SUPER_PARTITION_SIZE ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>fastboot</em></strong> and <strong><em>fastbootd</em></strong> comand  with <code>fastboot getvar all</code>.</p>
<details><summary>Click to open `fastboot getvar all` example</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://gist.github.com/assets/25868008/a2249ff6-6c45-44a8-85a7-862b756dda3b"><img src="https://gist.github.com/assets/25868008/a2249ff6-6c45-44a8-85a7-862b756dda3b" alt="ksnip_20240126-214105" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">In general, it is not necessary to write the size values for the System; Product; Vendor; Userdata; System_Ext; others; partitions because the code-tools of Custom Recovery find this part. So this becomes optional.</p>
<pre><code>  Use any online site that converts Hexadecimal to Decimal if you want to use Bytes.
Example: `boot:0x2000000` has 0x2000000 Hexadecimal and converted to Decimal has 33554432.
</code></pre>
<hr>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
</article>
  </div>

  </div>

</div>
