      <div id="file-3-1-boardconfig-mk_-md-readme" class="Box-body readme blob tmp-p-5 tmp-p-xl-6 gist-border-0" style="overflow: auto" tabindex="0" role="region" aria-label="3.1 BoardConfig.mk_.md content, created by lopestom on 02:09AM on January 24, 2024.">
    <article class="markdown-body entry-content container-lg" itemprop="text"><div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h5 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2.2.2.1 The *.mk files</h5><a id="user-content-2221-the-mk-files" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2.2.2.1 The *.mk files" href="#2221-the-mk-files"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<pre><code>part II of III
</code></pre>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h5 class="heading-element" dir="auto">1️⃣ BoardConfig.mk</h5><a id="user-content-one-boardconfigmk" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: :one: BoardConfig.mk" href="#one-boardconfigmk"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Kernel</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_KERNEL_IMAGE_NAME</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-v">Image</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">gz</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_PREBUILT_KERNEL</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">DEVICE_PATH</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">prebuilt</span>/<span class="pl-v">Image</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">gz</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_PREBUILT_DTB</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">DEVICE_PATH</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">prebuilt</span>/<span class="pl-en">dtb</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">img</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_PREBUILT_DTBOIMAGE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">DEVICE_PATH</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">prebuilt</span>/<span class="pl-en">dtbo</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">img</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_INCLUDE_DTB_IN_BOOTIMG</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_INCLUDE_RECOVERY_DTBO</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_FLASH_BLOCK_SIZE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">131072</span> <span class="pl-c"># (BOARD_KERNEL_PAGESIZE * 64)</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">This is a continuation part of <code># Kernel</code> setting fom here <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#221-split-or-files_info">2.2.1 Split or files_info</a>.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>BOARD_KERNEL_IMAGE_NAME ➡️ This is name of <em>kernel</em> to source code put in the correct path. You can use <code>zImage</code> or <code>Image.gz</code> for 32-bits devices. If you have doubts so put <code>kernel</code>.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>In some case the <em>kernel</em> and <em>dtb</em> is unique one file. So for that you can read the part of <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/3c1f3eaa66248c56e61acf19ddd4b96c#understanding-if-the-custom-recovery-device-tree-for-mediatek-devices-is-good-for-compiling">prebuilt</a>. Therefore when you use that so the <code>TARGET_PREBUILT_KERNEL</code> have name and <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/dto#load-dt" rel="nofollow">Image.gz-dtb</a> file.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>TARGET_PREBUILT_KERNEL ➡️ The source code include the <em>kernel</em> file within \Split folder of recovery_ramdisk.</li>
<li>TARGET_PREBUILT_DTB ➡️ The source code include the <em>dtb</em> file within \Split folder of recovery_ramdisk.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>In some case the <em>dtb</em> file is unpacked detailed - you can read the part of <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/3c1f3eaa66248c56e61acf19ddd4b96c#understanding-if-the-custom-recovery-device-tree-for-mediatek-devices-is-good-for-compiling">prebuilt -  In addition,</a> - and that need a special folder/directory <code>BOARD_PREBUILT_DTBIMAGE_DIR := prebuilt/dtb/mt6xxx.dtb</code> and the file will be build and copied by the source code to the correct path. Therefore when you use that so not write the <code>BOARD_INCLUDE_DTB_IN_BOOTIMG</code> variable.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>BOARD_PREBUILT_DTBOIMAGE ➡️ The source code include the <em>dtbo</em> file within \Split folder of recovery_ramdisk.</li>
</ul>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>In case the dt file is unpacked - you can read the part of <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/3c1f3eaa66248c56e61acf19ddd4b96c#understanding-if-the-custom-recovery-device-tree-for-mediatek-devices-is-good-for-compiling">prebuilt -  In addition,</a> - and that need a special folder/directory <code>BOARD_PREBUILT_DTBIMAGE_DIR := prebuilt/dtb/mt6xxx.dtb</code> and the file will be build and copied by the source code to the correct path. Therefore when you use that so not write the <code>BOARD_INCLUDE_DTB_IN_BOOTIMG</code> variable.</li>
</ul>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">BOARD_INCLUDE_DTB_IN_BOOTIMG ➡️ This allows for the inclusion of Device Tree Blob (DTB) files during the build process in the bootimage or recoveryimage or vendorbootimage.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">BOARD_INCLUDE_RECOVERY_DTBO ➡️ This allows for the inclusion of Device Tree Blob Overlay (DTBO) files during the build process in the recoveryimage.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">BOARD_FLASH_BLOCK_SIZE ➡️ Typically refers to the size of the flash memory block on the development board. The value of this variable depend of <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#221-split-or-files_info">BOARD_KERNEL_PAGESIZE</a> value and if the device have 32-bits or 64-bits.</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th align="center">BOARD_KERNEL_PAGESIZE</th>
<th align="center">32-bits</th>
<th align="center">64-bits</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td align="center">2048</td>
<td align="center">65536</td>
<td align="center">131072</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td align="center">4096</td>
<td align="center">131072</td>
<td align="center">262144</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c1">TARGET_KERNEL_ARCH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">arm64</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_KERNEL_HEADER_ARCH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">arm64</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>TARGET_KERNEL_ARCH & TARGET_KERNEL_HEADER_ARCH ➡️ Specific part of <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#one-boardconfigmk"># Architecture</a> similar to <code>TARGET_ARCH</code>. It determines the system-specific features, instructions  and optimizations.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Workaround for error copying vendor files to recovery ramdisk</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_COPY_OUT_VENDOR</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">vendor</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>This will create a folder for certain required partition. But to avoid errors, it is necessary to have this partition specified in <code>File systems and partitions</code> part.</li>
<li>Example:
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># File systems and partitions</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_SYSTEM_EXTIMAGE_FILE_SYSTEM_TYPE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">ext4</span>

<span class="pl-c"># Workaround for error copying vendor files to recovery ramdisk</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_COPY_OUT_SYSTEM_EXT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">system_ext</span></pre></div>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c1">TARGET_USERIMAGES_USE_EXT4</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_USERIMAGES_USE_F2FS</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>TARGET_USERIMAGES_USE_EXT4 ➡️ This configuration setting specifies the system for managger userimages partition should formatted and build using the EXT4 fstype.</li>
<li>TARGET_USERIMAGES_USE_F2FS ➡️ This configuration setting specifies the system for managger userimages partition should formatted and build using the F2FS fstype.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># System as root</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_BUILD_SYSTEM_ROOT_IMAGE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">false</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_SUPPRESS_SECURE_ERASE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>BOARD_BUILD_SYSTEM_ROOT_IMAGE ➡️ For the A10+ the AOSP source code limited the use of SAR (System As ROOT = /system_root/system/). The Custom Recovery mount the system image as <code>/</code>. Therefore it must be set to false.</li>
<li>BOARD_SUPPRESS_SECURE_ERASE ➡️ Secure erase is a security feature that permanently deletes all data from a device to prevent unauthorized access or data recovery..</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># AVB</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_AVB_ENABLE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>The AVB is <a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform/external/avb/+/master/README.md#android-verified-boot-2_0" rel="nofollow">Android Verified Boot</a> and there are many websites that explain its in detail. So there is no need to delve into it. But you might be interested in something so read <a href="https://static.linaro.org/connect/san19/presentations/san19-217.pdf" rel="nofollow">New Android requirements for bootloaders</a> on page 15.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>The most important thing in this part is to know the value of <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#221-split-or-files_info">BOARD_KERNEL_PAGESIZE</a>. If you are unsure if you want to use this or how to use this, then you can copy from any device tree that have img file compiled successfully.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Hack: prevent anti rollback</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PLATFORM_SECURITY_PATCH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">2099</span>-<span class="pl-c1">12</span>-<span class="pl-c1">31</span>
<span class="pl-c1">VENDOR_SECURITY_PATCH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">PLATFORM_SECURITY_PATCH</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOOT_SECURITY_PATCH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">PLATFORM_SECURITY_PATCH</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PLATFORM_VERSION</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">99.87</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">36</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PLATFORM_VERSION_LAST_STABLE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">PLATFORM_VERSION</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>PLATFORM_SECURITY_PATCH ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by <code>ro.build.version.security_patch=</code> line.</li>
<li>VENDOR_SECURITY_PATCH ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by <code>ro.vendor.build.security_patch=</code> line.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Resuming, with a future date means there are no problems with <a href="https://www.psacertified.org/blog/anti-rollback-explained/" rel="nofollow">Rollback</a>. But on some devices, depending on the encryption setting, the VENDOR_SECURITY_PATCH variable must have the same date.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>BOOT_SECURITY_PATCH ➡️ Comes from <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#221-split-or-files_info">BOARD_OS_PATCH_LEVEL</a> of Split or files_info file line.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>On newer devices with kernel version 5.xx you can read more in the <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/kernel/gki-android13-5_10-release-builds#boot-image-os-version-spl" rel="nofollow">Boot image OS version and SPL</a>.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>PLATFORM_VERSION ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by <code>ro.build.version.release=</code> line.</li>
<li>PLATFORM_VERSION_LAST_STABLE ➡️ Maybe comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by <code>ro.build.version.release_or_codename=</code> line.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">If there is a line with the word <em>stable</em> or <em>last</em>, great. But usually <a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform//build/+/80ab21490b7e67b4c5d2a2a126d7bedba42b6257/core/version_defaults.mk#88" rel="nofollow">there isn't</a>. On newer devices with kernel version 5.xx usually with Android 12, the system version is updated and its differ, written as Android 13. Then you must confirm <code>ro.build.version.release_or_codename=</code> in the <strong><em>build.prop</em></strong> file of system.img file.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">If you are unsure if you want to use this or <a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform//build/+/80ab21490b7e67b4c5d2a2a126d7bedba42b6257/core/version_defaults.mk#134" rel="nofollow">how to use this</a>, then you can copy from any device tree that have img file compiled successfully.</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Metadata</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_USES_METADATA_PARTITION</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_ROOT_EXTRA_FOLDERS</span> += <span class="pl-en">metadata</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>BOARD_USES_METADATA_PARTITION ➡️ Comes from <strong>/ramdisk/system/etc/<code>recovery.fstab</code></strong> stock recovery_ramdisk file by mountpoint. You must look if a <code>\metadata</code> partition exists.</li>
<li>BOARD_ROOT_EXTRA_FOLDERS ➡️ This variable is used to create folders within of <code>\ramdisk\</code> when compiling the Custom recovery with cases where there are no files inside them. With device tree in the <code>device_brand_devicename/recovery/root/</code>, the source code cannot create folders if not have files inside them.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>You can write more folders if need. Example: <code>BOARD_ROOT_EXTRA_FOLDERS += metadata transf</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Crypto</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_INCLUDE_CRYPTO</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_INCLUDE_CRYPTO_FBE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_INCLUDE_FBE_METADATA_DECRYPT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_USE_FSCRYPT_POLICY</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">2</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_PREPARE_DATA_MEDIA_EARLY</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>

<span class="pl-c"># Additional binaries & libraries needed for recovery</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_RECOVERY_DEVICE_MODULES</span> += <span class="pl-en">libkeymaster4</span>

<span class="pl-c1">TW_RECOVERY_ADDITIONAL_RELINK_LIBRARY_FILES</span> += $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">TARGET_OUT_SHARED_LIBRARIES</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">libkeymaster4</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">so</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">Just pay attention to what the variable or flag is for. For encryption and decryption files and processes, there is another document for us to understand about how it works.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_INCLUDE_CRYPTO ➡️ It will enable encryption and decryption related processes and files.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>If you not know about that so change <code>true</code>to <code>false</code>.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_INCLUDE_CRYPTO_FBE ➡️ It will enable encryption and decryption related processes and files for <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/security/features/encryption" rel="nofollow">FBE</a> option.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Resuming, the source code will compile and copy the files into the Custom Recovery <code>\ramdisk\system\bin</code> and <code>\ramdisk\system\lib_lib64</code> folders.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_INCLUDE_FBE_METADATA_DECRYPT ➡️ It will enable encryption and decryption related processes and files if Metadata has encryption mode.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_USE_FSCRYPT_POLICY ➡️ This will be different for devices coming with version 1 (v1) or version 2 (v2) options related to encryption and decryption. The source code will compile files and include information relating to each version. We usually know about this by looking at the <code>\data</code> partition in the <strong>/ramdisk/system/etc/<code>recovery.fstab</code></strong> stock recovery_ramdisk file by flags. Note that only version 2 will be written <em>v2</em> and therefore if this is not available, the option is version 1.</p>
<details><summary>Click to open recovery.fstab example</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://gist.github.com/assets/25868008/feb3d456-f040-426e-a1a9-6060846f11b4"><img src="https://gist.github.com/assets/25868008/feb3d456-f040-426e-a1a9-6060846f11b4" alt="ksnip_20240128-140332" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_PREPARE_DATA_MEDIA_EARLY ➡️ Maybe this <a href="https://github.com/TeamWin/android_bootable_recovery/blob/8eade06b98f2f009ebbad5640107297865b90dc6/partition.cpp#L1269C10-L1269C37">variable</a> should relink /data/media/0 to /data. Solve specific decryption problems on some devices.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TARGET_RECOVERY_DEVICE_MODULES & TW_RECOVERY_ADDITIONAL_RELINK_LIBRARY_FILES ➡️ This variable tells you to compile a specific or necessary file that the Custom Recovery source code does not create. For convenience, a few years ago, the code was not yet updated and so the device tree needed extra files for the encryption/decryption process. This variable is not just for encryption/decryption and therefore you can "call" any file for compilation as long as it is in the Android source code and its specific version according to the Custom Recovery repository (minimal manifest).</p>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># fastbootd A10+ ↪️ Additional binaries & libraries needed when have recovery.img</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_RECOVERY_DEVICE_MODULES</span> += \
          <span class="pl-en">ashmemd_aidl_interface</span>-<span class="pl-en">cpp</span> \
          <span class="pl-en">libashmemd_client</span>

<span class="pl-c1">TW_RECOVERY_ADDITIONAL_RELINK_LIBRARY_FILES</span> += \
$<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">TARGET_OUT_SHARED_LIBRARIES</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">libashmemd_client</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">so</span> \
$<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">TARGET_OUT_SHARED_LIBRARIES</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">ashmemd_aidl_interface</span>-<span class="pl-en">cpp</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">so</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">TARGET_RECOVERY_DEVICE_MODULES & TW_RECOVERY_ADDITIONAL_RELINK_LIBRARY_FILES ➡️ This variable tells you to compile a specific or necessary file that the Custom Recovery source code does not create. The <code>ashmemd</code> module files are required to start fastbootd when the device has the recovery.img partition active and starting with Android 10+.</p>
<p dir="auto">AOSP Source code moules for <a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform/system/ashmemd/+/768e1bc76df9e2f5fb4653d1dffaf0c7ceb68f70" rel="nofollow">ashmemd</a>.</p>
<p dir="auto">LOS Source code moules for <a href="https://review.lineageos.org/c/LineageOS/android_device_cyanogen_msm8916-common/+/312022/1" rel="nofollow">ashmemd</a>.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Ashmemd is a tool in Android that manages shared memory between processes. It stands for "Anonymous Shared Memory Device". It allows processes to allocate and share memory without the need for file-backed memory mappings. This can be useful for inter-process communication and sharing data efficiently between processes.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Recovery</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_RECOVERY_PIXEL_FORMAT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s">"BGRA_8888"</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_RECOVERY_FSTAB</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">DEVICE_PATH</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">recovery</span>/<span class="pl-en">root</span>/<span class="pl-en">system</span>/<span class="pl-en">etc</span>/<span class="pl-en">recovery</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">fstab</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_HAS_NO_SELECT_BUTTON</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">RECOVERY_SDCARD_ON_DATA</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>TARGET_RECOVERY_PIXEL_FORMAT ➡️ This variable depends on how your device was configured to show certain colors in the screen. So there are some options like: <code>RGB_565</code>; <code>RGB_888;</code> <code>RGBA_8888</code>; <code>RGBX_8888</code>; <code>BGRA_8888</code>; <code>BGRX_8888</code>; <code>ABGR_8888</code>; <code>RGBA_5551</code>; <code>RGBA_4444</code>.</li>
<li>TARGET_RECOVERY_FSTAB ➡️ This variable is used to indicate the folder that the recovery.fstab file is in and is necessary when Custom Recovery starts, so it must mount main partitions. You can leave the recovery.fstab file anywhere, as long as you write the local in the variable. Even so, it will be automatically copied to the <code>\recovery_ramdisk\system\etc</code> folder in compiled Custom Recovery file.</li>
<li>BOARD_HAS_NO_SELECT_BUTTON ➡️ In short, most Custom Recoveries do not use physical buttons to select and confirm options on their interface. Even if your device has the three lower buttons, they will only serve the same Custom Recovery option (if any) such as <em>Back</em>, <em>Menu</em> and <em>Show Processin the Screen</em>.
I only know of some like the current LOS Recovery and other old ones like CWM, Carliv, Philz and Dingdoing that need to use buttons like <em>Power</em>, <em>Vol -</em> and <em>Vol +</em> for certain actions.</li>
<li>RECOVERY_SDCARD_ON_DATA ➡️ This enables proper handling of /data/media on devices that have this folder for storage.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Properties</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_SYSTEM_PROP</span> += $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">DEVICE_PATH</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">system</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">prop</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_VENDOR_PROP</span> += $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">DEVICE_PATH</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">prop</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_RECOVERY_INITRC</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">DEVICE_PATH</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">recovery</span>/<span class="pl-en">root</span>/<span class="pl-en">init</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">recovery</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">mt6762</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">rc</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>TARGET_SYSTEM_PROP ➡️ This variable is used to copy property lines coming from the system.img\build.prop part that are relevant for certain functions to work. With the device tree in <strong>device_brand_devicename/<code>system.prop</code></strong>, the text inside the system.prop file will be copied in the Custom Recovery compiled file to the folder with the <strong>\recovery_ramdisk\<code>prop.default</code></strong> file.</li>
<li>TARGET_VENDOR_PROP ➡️ This variable is used to copy property lines coming from the vendor.img\build.prop part that are relevant for certain functions to work. With the device tree in <strong>device_brand_devicename/<code>vendor.prop</code></strong>, the text inside the vendor.prop file will be copied in the Custom Recovery compiled file to the folder with the <strong>\recovery_ramdisk\<code>prop.default</code></strong> file.</li>
<li>TARGET_RECOVERY_INITRC ➡️ Use a custom init.rc file in the Custom Recovery to start necessary and/or special services and processes.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>The init.recovery.{hardware}.rc file can also be seen as init.recovery.{platform}.rc and will serve to indicate the file paths, as well as to start the encryption/decryption mode process.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Treble</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_VNDK_VERSION</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">current</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/vndk/build-system#configuring-build-support" rel="nofollow">BOARD_VNDK_VERSION</a> ➡️ When this variable is set, all modules are checked. Enabling that, the build system enforces the <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/vndk/enabling#enabling" rel="nofollow">dependency and header</a> requirements.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
</article>
  </div>

  </div>

</div>

      <div id="file-3-2-boardconfig-mk_-md-readme" class="Box-body readme blob tmp-p-5 tmp-p-xl-6 gist-border-0" style="overflow: auto" tabindex="0" role="region" aria-label="3.2 BoardConfig.mk_.md content, created by lopestom on 02:09AM on January 24, 2024.">
    <article class="markdown-body entry-content container-lg" itemprop="text"><div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h5 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2.2.2.1 The *.mk files</h5><a id="user-content-2221-the-mk-files" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2.2.2.1 The *.mk files" href="#2221-the-mk-files"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<pre><code>part III of III
</code></pre>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h5 class="heading-element" dir="auto">1️⃣ BoardConfig.mk</h5><a id="user-content-one-boardconfigmk" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: :one: BoardConfig.mk" href="#one-boardconfigmk"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Display</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_BRIGHTNESS_PATH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-sr">/sys/class</span>/<span class="pl-en">leds</span>/<span class="pl-en">lcd</span>-<span class="pl-en">backlight</span>/<span class="pl-en">brightness</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_RECOVERY_LCD_BACKLIGHT_PATH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> \"/<span class="pl-en">sys</span>/<span class="pl-k">class</span><span class="pl-sr">/leds/lcd</span>-<span class="pl-en">backlight</span>/<span class="pl-en">brightness</span>\"
<span class="pl-c1">TW_CUSTOM_CPU_TEMP_PATH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-sr">/sys/devices</span>/<span class="pl-en">virtual</span>/<span class="pl-en">thermal</span>/<span class="pl-en">thermal_zone1</span>/<span class="pl-en">temp</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_MAX_BRIGHTNESS</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">255</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_DEFAULT_BRIGHTNESS</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">150</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_NO_SCREEN_BLANK</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_SCREEN_BLANK_ON_BOOT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_INPUT_BLACKLIST</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s">"hbtp_vm"</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">This part will define some hardware parts related to the screen configuration that your device has so that Custom Recovery informs and displays the information correctly. This can be used to set a nicer graphics mode and or fix presentation issues.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_BRIGHTNESS_PATH ➡️ Comes from <code>/sys/class/leds/*</code> for brightness works correctlly in the screen.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TARGET_RECOVERY_LCD_BACKLIGHT_PATH ➡️ Comes from <code>/sys/class/leds/*</code> for LCD works correctlly in the screen.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_CUSTOM_CPU_TEMP_PATH ➡️ Comes from <code>/sys/devices/virtual/thermal*</code> for show the CPU Temperature (thermal) ºC info in the screen.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_NO_SCREEN_BLANK ➡️ Don't blank screen (available optional inside recovery settings too).</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_SCREEN_BLANK_ON_BOOT ➡️ It will jolt the screen/touch driver. If on the default settings the screen will only kick in to action/start AFTER it has gone to sleep once and has been woken up again with a key press, this will be needed to set to <code>true</code>. There is still a <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/685cdd9c71476e9daf95ca612066e23f#now-everyone-who-is-going-to-collect-compile-or-porting-custom-recovery-should-pay-attention-to-firmware-files">screen touch issue</a> if this option is set to <code>true</code>.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>On some legacy devices, the Custom Recovery will not boot without this flag.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_INPUT_BLACKLIST ➡️ It is used to indicate that a particular function is prohibited from access or use something because interfere with other anything. That allows users to blacklist certain input values or characters. When this setting is enabled, reject any input that contains blacklisted values or characters, preventing them from being processed further.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_MAX_BRIGHTNESS ➡️ Used to set the maximum brightness level to be displayed in the screen.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_DEFAULT_BRIGHTNESS ➡️ Sets the default brightness level to be used when Custom Recovery starts.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">If you don't know the correct path, then remove any variables so there are no errors. In this sense, the Custom Recovery code itself tries to find the correct path directly on the device. There are some flags written with <code>_CUSTOM_</code> and this serves to quickly indicate a path where the Custom Recovery code was unable to obtain information. Therefore, defining it exclusively serves to confirm the correct path. <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/kernel/reqs-interfaces#sysfs" rel="nofollow">Some informations</a> about <code>\sys</code>.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">There are flags (TW_NO_*) that disable the source code from finding some functions. Example: <code>TW_NO_BATT_PERCENT := true</code> Disables the display of the battery percentage for devices that don't support it properly.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">Use the adb tool for <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#file-2-mediatek-devices-vs-knowledge-md">have informations</a> from your device. Use this command: <code>adb shell su -c "ls -R" > info.txt</code> if you want. But look for have root priviledges or Root acess whit any apk process.</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Resolution</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_THEME</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">portrait_hdpi</span>
<span class="pl-c1">DEVICE_SCREEN_WIDTH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">720</span>
<span class="pl-c1">DEVICE_SCREEN_HEIGHT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">1600</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>TW_THEME ➡️ Set your device's resolution mode with correct presentation and rotation in the screen.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>There are still customized or exclusive themes, but this is another subject.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>DEVICE_SCREEN_WIDTH ➡️ Enter the Screen Width value according to the resolution of your device.</li>
<li>DEVICE_SCREEN_HEIGHT ➡️ Enter the Screen Height value according to the resolution of your device.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Statusbar icons flags 720 x 1600</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_STATUS_ICONS_ALIGN</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">center</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_CUSTOM_CPU_POS</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">142</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_CUSTOM_CLOCK_POS</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">330</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_CUSTOM_BATTERY_POS</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">800</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">This part will define how the upper part (Battery Level, Clockstamp and CPU:Temperature °C) with <a href="https://github.com/TeamWin/android_bootable_recovery/commit/09badaed48e57c3fadc63922b77e5087666a54c2">information</a> will be shown, and can be customized due to user choice or particularities, such as the location (POSition) of the camera cutout.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://github.com/TeamWin/android_bootable_recovery/commit/fcbaf814f3d46e21ac7ffa77f4883228468c1d2a">TW_STATUS_ICONS_ALIGN</a> ➡️ Option to align information, therefore how it will be displayed on the screen.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_CUSTOM_CPU_POS ➡️ Option to align CPU:Temperature °C information according to the location value on the screen.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_CUSTOM_CLOCK_POS ➡️ Option to align Clockstamp information according to the location value on the screen.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_CUSTOM_BATTERY_POS ➡️ Option to align Battery Level % information according to the location value on the screen.</p>
<details><summary>Click to open - Some Examples</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://gist.github.com/assets/25868008/f11bc62b-fe4c-4d3c-ac9b-961551617d2b"><img src="https://gist.github.com/assets/25868008/f11bc62b-fe4c-4d3c-ac9b-961551617d2b" alt="Example POS" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Has other flags. This is optional and depends on each case. <code>Maybe it's deprecated?!</code></p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c1">TW_H_OFFSET</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_Y_OFFSET</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_X_OFFSET</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_W_OFFSET</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>TW_H_OFFSET ➡️ value of the Height axis displacement.</li>
<li>TW_Y_OFFSET ➡️ value Y from Axis offset for borked displays.</li>
<li>TW_X_OFFSET ➡️ value X from Axis offset for borked displays.</li>
<li>TW_W_OFFSET ➡️ value of the Width axis displacement.
Example:
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre>   <span class="pl-c1">TW_Y_OFFSET</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">90</span>
   <span class="pl-c1">TW_H_OFFSET</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> -<span class="pl-c1">90</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>The value depends on the resolution and you can see the black part from top to bottom in the 2 photos in Some Examples.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># TWRP specific build flags - Configuration</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_USE_MODEL_HARDWARE_ID_FOR_DEVICE_ID</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_USE_TOOLBOX</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_EXTRA_LANGUAGES</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">false</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_DEFAULT_LANGUAGE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">en</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_EXCLUDE_DEFAULT_USB_INIT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_INCLUDE_NTFS_3G</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_USES_MKE2FS</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_DISABLE_TRIPLE_BUFFERING</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">false</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>TW_USE_MODEL_HARDWARE_ID_FOR_DEVICE_ID ➡️ On some devices, the TWRP backup folder name will show <code>0000000000</code> because the CPU information has no serial number or <code>123456789ABCDEF</code> if lost/erased. Using this flag it will use <code>ro.product.model</code> as the TWRP backup folder name.</li>
<li>TW_USE_TOOLBOX ➡️ Choose to compile and use ToyBox instead of Busybox.</li>
<li>TW_EXTRA_LANGUAGES ➡️ Add translated files for languages generally from the Asian region {Japanese, Chinese (Simplified), Chinese (Traditional) and Sinhala (Sri Lanka)}.</li>
<li>TW_DEFAULT_LANGUAGE ➡️ Sets the language when Custom Recovery starts on the device.</li>
<li>TW_EXCLUDE_DEFAULT_USB_INIT ➡️ Choose it not include the default init.recovery.usb.rc file (from source code), so need must provide your own (from device tree) or use the required definitions within the init.recovery.{hardware}.rc file.</li>
<li>TW_INCLUDE_NTFS_3G ➡️ It's include a NTFS driver file in the Custom Recovery. NTFS-3G allows for read and write access to NTFS partitions, commonly used by Windows OS.</li>
<li>TARGET_USES_MKE2FS ➡️ It will compile the files needed to use the "mke2fs" command to create an ext2, ext3 or ext4 filesystem.</li>
<li>TARGET_DISABLE_TRIPLE_BUFFERING ➡️ Set <code>true</code> to disable and <code>false</code> to not disable. Triple buffering is a technique used in computer graphics to reduce screen tearing and improve overall performance by using three buffers instead of two.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Storage</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_NO_USB_STORAGE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">false</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_INTERNAL_STORAGE_PATH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s">"/data/media/0"</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_INTERNAL_STORAGE_MOUNT_POINT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s">"data"</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_EXTERNAL_STORAGE_PATH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s">"/external_sd"</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_EXTERNAL_STORAGE_MOUNT_POINT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s">"external_sd"</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_HAS_MTP</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_MTP_DEVICE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/mtp</span><span class="pl-en">_usb</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_USE_CUSTOM_LUN_FILE_PATH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-sr">/config/usb</span><span class="pl-en">_gadget</span>/<span class="pl-en">g1</span>/<span class="pl-en">functions</span>/<span class="pl-en">mass_storage</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">usb0</span>/<span class="pl-en">lun</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span>%<span class="pl-en">d</span>/<span class="pl-en">file</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>TW_NO_USB_STORAGE ➡️ Removes the USB storage button on devices that don't support USB storage.</li>
<li>TW_INTERNAL_STORAGE_PATH & TW_INTERNAL_STORAGE_MOUNT_POINT & TW_EXTERNAL_STORAGE_PATH & TW_EXTERNAL_STORAGE_MOUNT_POINT ➡️ Specify the dual storage partition (internal and external) for devices that have it. The mount point must match something in the files <code>\ramdisk\system\etc\recovery.fstab</code> or <code>\device_brand_devicename\recovery\root\system\etc\twrp.fstab_flag</code> or any correct storage mount point information.</li>
<li>TW_HAS_MTP ➡️ Indicates that Custom Recovery will support Media Transfer Protocol (MTP) for transferring files between the device and a computer. Make sure the device offers MTP. Check the stock init.{hardware}.usb file.</li>
<li>TARGET_USE_CUSTOM_LUN_FILE_PATH ➡️ Specify a custom path to the lun file on device that directing to internal storage.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Debug</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TWRP_INCLUDE_LOGCAT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_USES_LOGD</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>TWRP_INCLUDE_LOGCAT ➡️ The source code of Custom Recovery include logcat output. It's useful for debugging and troubleshooting issues in the recovery image. This allows developers to access the logcat logs from within Custom Recovery, providing additional information about device system events, errors, and crashes.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Including logcat output in the recovery image can make the image larger, so it is recommended to only enable this option for debugging purposes and disable it for release builds.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>TARGET_USES_LOGD ➡️ Uses the logd log buffer for logging messages which can be accessed using the "logcat" command.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Excludes</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_EXCLUDE_TWRPAPP</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_EXCLUDE_APEX</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Any TW_EXCLUDE_* flag ➡️ It serves to avoid adding certain functions and thus the processes & files resulting from them. Remove/Exclude anything no need and or to have minimal img file size.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Firmware version & Developer Display name</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_DEFAULT_DEVICE_NAME</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">fpluspro_H166</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_DEVICE_VERSION</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">2</span>-<span class="pl-en">fpluspro_H166_SW09</span> - <span class="pl-en">lopestom</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_DEFAULT_DEVICE_NAME ➡️ Represents the default name assigned to a device when it is built with TWRP source code. The value of this variable is typically set to the device code name or model name or custom name to provide an identifiable name for the recovery image.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_DEVICE_VERSION ➡️ Used to show the author/developer name or custom name value for a specific device version, or to specify compatibility requirements for a specific firmware version. The value will localized below the Custom Recovery name in the main page's action bar and also on the splashscreen below of Custom Recovery version.</p>
<details><summary>Click to open the Example</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://gist.github.com/assets/25868008/a3e89d69-22ee-4b17-b298-ca5ed1d48d11"><img src="https://gist.github.com/assets/25868008/a3e89d69-22ee-4b17-b298-ca5ed1d48d11" alt="IMG_2023-24" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>For some custom recoveries, this flag does not work because it depends of maintainer's choice on the Custom Recovery source code. Other cases depend on the custom theme used and the value cannot be shown.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h5 class="heading-element" dir="auto">1️⃣ BoardConfig.mk for Recovery_ramdisk in the boot.img</h5><a id="user-content-one-boardconfigmk-for-recovery_ramdisk-in-the-bootimg" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: :one: BoardConfig.mk for Recovery_ramdisk in the boot.img" href="#one-boardconfigmk-for-recovery_ramdisk-in-the-bootimg"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">This is a separate part for a special reason and will not be the only one.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>In the firmware file, the recovery.img file does not exist, just look for the <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/ramdisk-partitions#non-ab-boot-img" rel="nofollow">boot.img file</a>. So <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/ramdisk-partitions" rel="nofollow">Recovery_ramdisk</a> is inside boot.img file and the filesystem mountpoint certainly does not contain the \recovery<sup><a href="#user-content-fn-%C2%B9-96c048dd4f1699d44cfb72f80bdf1597" id="user-content-fnref-%c2%b9-96c048dd4f1699d44cfb72f80bdf1597" data-footnote-ref="" aria-describedby="footnote-label">1</a></sup> partition. So the Split or files_info part will not have the dtbo<sup><a href="#user-content-fn-%C2%B2-96c048dd4f1699d44cfb72f80bdf1597" id="user-content-fnref-%c2%b2-96c048dd4f1699d44cfb72f80bdf1597" data-footnote-ref="" aria-describedby="footnote-label">2</a></sup> file.</li>
</ul>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Kernel</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_KERNEL_CMDLINE</span> += <span class="pl-en">androidboot</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">force_normal_boot</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_BOOT_HEADER_VERSION</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">2</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">androidboot.force_normal_boot=1 ➡️ Added for Android 10+ Boot <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/bootloader/boot-reason#boot-reasons" rel="nofollow">Reason</a> or <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/ota/dynamic_partitions/implement#system-as-root-changes" rel="nofollow">System-as-root</a> changes.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Resuming, when you choose Reboot to System option, then bootloader can skip recovery and boot normal Android.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">BOARD_BOOT_HEADER_VERSION ➡️ Note that it is with version 2. I am repeating it just to make it clear that there is no version 1 currently for recovery_ramdisk for the boot.img file.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Note that there is no reference to RECOVERY related to the partition and its size. Understand that's the part that will tell the Custom Recovery source code about the location of the recovery_ramdisk. Therefore, use the previous settings and flags, but without RECOVERY to uses to build the recovery.img file.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Recovery</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_USES_RECOVERY_AS_BOOT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_NO_RECOVERY</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_HAS_NO_RECOVERY_PARTITION</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">BOARD_USES_RECOVERY_AS_BOOT ➡️ This variable indicates whether the device uses the recovery_ramdisk part in the <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/generic-boot#board-configurations" rel="nofollow">boot image</a>.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/generic-boot#setting-up-devices" rel="nofollow">Setup instructions</a> differ between devices launching with Android 12 or 13 and legacy devices.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Not use <code>BOARD_RECOVERYIMAGE_PARTITION_SIZE</code> variable!</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform/build/+/37b96ef%5E%21/" rel="nofollow">TARGET_NO_RECOVERY</a> ➡️ No build the recovery.img file. Strongly recommended for A/B target devices.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_HAS_NO_RECOVERY_PARTITION ➡️ To avoid the error about /boot partition not found. Required on devices without /recovery partition.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>BOOT/RAMDISK also exists and contains the first stage ramdisk if not using BOARD_BUILD_SYSTEM_ROOT_IMAGE.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Tools - magiskboot</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_INCLUDE_REPACKTOOLS</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">TW_INCLUDE_REPACKTOOLS ➡️ Compiles and creates the magiskboot file so that Custom Recovery has the <em><strong><em>Install Recovery Ramdisk</em></strong></em> option in the <strong>Advanced</strong> tab to install recovery.lz4.cpio on the recovery ramdisk part in the boot_$slot partition.</p>
<details><summary>Click to open the Example</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/25868008/301971650-5e8a8876-6464-4013-93b0-2ea5f1627ed5.jpg?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..ex5vGjCM0_znF3mG8bXAULXG0Zo3zGoDPTO1EZeX618"><img src="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/25868008/301971650-5e8a8876-6464-4013-93b0-2ea5f1627ed5.jpg?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..ex5vGjCM0_znF3mG8bXAULXG0Zo3zGoDPTO1EZeX618" alt="Advanced_Recovery_Ramdisk_resized" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<hr>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h5 class="heading-element" dir="auto">1️⃣ BoardConfig.mk for Recovery_ramdisk in the vendor_boot.img</h5><a id="user-content-one-boardconfigmk-for-recovery_ramdisk-in-the-vendor_bootimg" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: :one: BoardConfig.mk for Recovery_ramdisk in the vendor_boot.img" href="#one-boardconfigmk-for-recovery_ramdisk-in-the-vendor_bootimg"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">This is a separate part for a special reason and will not be the only one.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>In the firmware file, the recovery.img file does not exist. But like every Android, the boot.img file will be on it. The big difference is that if you find the <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/vendor-boot-partitions" rel="nofollow">vendor_boot</a>.img file in the firmware file, then you have the Recovery_ramdisk in the <code>\vendor_boot</code> partition. The filesystem mount point certainly does not contain the \recovery partition. Therefore, the Split or files_info part will not have the kernel<sup><a href="#user-content-fn-%C2%B3-96c048dd4f1699d44cfb72f80bdf1597" id="user-content-fnref-%c2%b3-96c048dd4f1699d44cfb72f80bdf1597" data-footnote-ref="" aria-describedby="footnote-label">3</a></sup> files and also the dtbo.</li>
</ul>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Architecture</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_2ND_ARCH_VARIANT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">armv8</span>-<span class="pl-c1">2</span><span class="pl-en">a</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>TARGET_2ND_ARCH_VARIANT ➡️ It's implemented just the ARMv8 ABI saying update <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#one-boardconfigmk">Architecture</a> CPU.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Don't be surprised, they will soon release ARMv9.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Kernel</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_NO_KERNEL</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_VENDOR_CMDLINE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">bootopt</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">64</span><span class="pl-c1">S3</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-c1">32</span><span class="pl-c1">N2</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-c1">64</span><span class="pl-c1">N2</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_PAGE_SIZE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">4096</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_BOOT_HEADER_VERSION</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">4</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_HEADER_SIZE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">2128</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_FLASH_BLOCK_SIZE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">262144</span> <span class="pl-c">## (BOARD_PAGE_SIZE * 64).</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_PREBUILT_DTB</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">DEVICE_PATH</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">prebuilt</span>/<span class="pl-en">dtb</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_DTB_SIZE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">191249</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_DTB_OFFSET</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">0x07c88000</span>

<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_KERNEL_SEPARATED_DTBO</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_RAMDISK_USE_LZ4</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>

<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS</span> += --<span class="pl-en">dtb</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">TARGET_PREBUILT_DTB</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS</span> += --<span class="pl-en">vendor_cmdline</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">BOARD_VENDOR_CMDLINE</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS</span> += --<span class="pl-en">pagesize</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">BOARD_PAGE_SIZE</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span> --<span class="pl-en">board</span> <span class="pl-s">""</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS</span> += --<span class="pl-en">header_version</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">BOARD_BOOT_HEADER_VERSION</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS</span> += --<span class="pl-en">dtb_offset</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">BOARD_DTB_OFFSET</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/generic-boot#board-configurations" rel="nofollow">TARGET_NO_KERNEL</a> ➡️ This variable indicates if the build uses a prebuilt boot image. Setting <code>true</code> indicates that the kernel file is not needed.</li>
<li>BOARD_VENDOR_CMDLINE ➡️ Just a name change for correct partition identification.</li>
<li>BOARD_PAGE_SIZE ➡️ Change made for devices that have [<strong>only 64-bits</strong>] according to the new flash memory block.</li>
<li>BOARD_BOOT_HEADER_VERSION ➡️ <a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform/system/tools/mkbootimg/+/refs/heads/master/include/bootimg/bootimg.h#331" rel="nofollow">Updated</a> header for vendor_boot, perhaps related to kernel 5.1.xxx</li>
<li>BOARD_HEADER_SIZE ➡️ It <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/vendor-boot-partitions#vendor-boot-partition-v4" rel="nofollow">comes from</a> the unpacked img file information.</li>
<li>BOARD_FLASH_BLOCK_SIZE ➡️ According the <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#one-boardconfigmk">part II of III</a> table.</li>
<li>TARGET_PREBUILT_DTB ➡️ According the <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#one-boardconfigmk">part II of III</a> in the <code># Kernel</code> setting because the vendor_boot partition <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/vendor-boot-partitions#bootloader-support" rel="nofollow">contains</a> only dtb file.</li>
<li>BOARD_DTB_SIZE & BOARD_DTB_OFFSET  ➡️ It comes from the unpacked img file information.</li>
<li>BOARD_KERNEL_SEPARATED_DTBO ➡️ You can see in the firmware file the <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/dto/partitions" rel="nofollow">dtbo.img</a> file. In the unpacked vendor_boot.img you can see only the dtb file. So need inform the source code to not compile <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/dto/compile#implementing-the-dtbo-partition" rel="nofollow">dtbo</a> file.</li>
<li>BOARD_RAMDISK_USE_LZ4 ➡️ Better compression of the img file where the kernel has this capability.</li>
<li>BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS ➡️ Each part of <code>BOARD_*</code> needs BOARD_MKBOOTIMG_ARGS for the source code to create a file with the size value property defined.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># AVB</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_AVB_ENABLE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_AVB_MAKE_VBMETA_IMAGE_ARGS</span> += --<span class="pl-en">flags</span> <span class="pl-c1">3</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_AVB_ROLLBACK_INDEX</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">PLATFORM_SECURITY_PATCH_TIMESTAMP</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_AVB_RECOVERY_ALGORITHM</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">SHA256_RSA4096</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_AVB_RECOVERY_KEY_PATH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">external</span>/<span class="pl-en">avb</span>/<span class="pl-en">test</span>/<span class="pl-en">data</span>/<span class="pl-en">testkey_rsa4096</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">pem</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_AVB_RECOVERY_ROLLBACK_INDEX</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">PLATFORM_SECURITY_PATCH_TIMESTAMP</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_AVB_RECOVERY_ROLLBACK_INDEX_LOCATION</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">1</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>BOARD_AVB_RECOVERY_ALGORITHM & BOARD_AVB_RECOVERY_KEY_PATH ➡️ According to the BOARD_PAGE_SIZE value and the  reference of AVB <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/generic-boot#chain-vbmeta" rel="nofollow">source code</a> configuration.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># File systems and partitions</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_VENDOR_BOOTIMAGE_PARTITION_SIZE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">67108864</span>

<span class="pl-c"># Build a separate vendor_dlkm partition</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_USES_VENDOR_DLKMIMAGE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_VENDOR_DLKMIMAGE_FILE_SYSTEM_TYPE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">ext4</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_COPY_OUT_VENDOR_DLKM</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">vendor_dlkm</span>

<span class="pl-c"># Build a separate odm_dlkm partition</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_USES_ODM_DLKMIMAGE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_ODM_DLKMIMAGE_FILE_SYSTEM_TYPE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">ext4</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_COPY_OUT_ODM_DLKM</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">odm_dlkm</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>BOARD_VENDOR_BOOTIMAGE_PARTITION_SIZE ➡️ The real size of vendor_boot.img file. You can put Hexadecimal or Decimal value.</li>
<li><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/vendor-odm-dlkm-partition" rel="nofollow">BOARD_USES_VENDOR_DLKMIMAGE & BOARD_USES_ODM_DLKMIMAGE</a> ➡️ Set <code>true</code> when the ramdisk\system\etc\<code>recovery.fstab</code> file in mount point has these two extra partitions.</li>
<li>TARGET_COPY_OUT_VENDOR_DLKM & TARGET_COPY_OUT_ODM_DLKM ➡️ For copying partition files to recovery ramdisk of Custom Recovery. Same process of TARGET_COPY_OUT_VENDOR in the <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#one-boardconfigmk">part II of III</a>.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Pay attention here to the fstype (ext4 or erofs) of the system, system_ext, product, vendor, vendor_dlkm, odm_dlkm partitions. Same as fstype of userdata partition (ext4 or f2fs).
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Example: <code>BOARD_SYSTEMIMAGE_FILE_SYSTEM_TYPE := erofs</code> and <code>BOARD_USERDATAIMAGE_FILE_SYSTEM_TYPE := f2fs</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Recovery</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TARGET_NO_RECOVERY</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_EXCLUDE_KERNEL_FROM_RECOVERY_IMAGE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_MOVE_RECOVERY_RESOURCES_TO_VENDOR_BOOT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">BOARD_INCLUDE_RECOVERY_RAMDISK_IN_VENDOR_BOOT</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">TARGET_NO_RECOVERY ➡️ Recommended when there is no \recovery partition.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/generic-boot#board-configurations" rel="nofollow">BOARD_EXCLUDE_KERNEL_FROM_RECOVERY_IMAGE</a> ➡️ This variable controls whether the recovery image contains a kernel or not.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/generic-boot#setting-board-values" rel="nofollow">BOARD_MOVE_RECOVERY_RESOURCES_TO_VENDOR_BOOT</a> ➡️ When set to <code>true</code>, recovery resources are built to vendor_ramdisk only and aren't built to recovery/root/.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/vendor-boot-partitions#build-support" rel="nofollow">BOARD_INCLUDE_RECOVERY_RAMDISK_IN_VENDOR_BOOT</a> ➡️ To build recovery resources as a standalone recovery ramdisk in vendor_boot.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Note that there is no reference to RECOVERY related to the partition and its size. Understand that's the part that will tell the Custom Recovery source code about the location of the recovery_ramdisk. Therefore, use the previous settings and flags, but <em><strong>without the BOARD_USES_RECOVERY_AS_BOOT</strong></em> variable as it is not necessary to compile the boot.img file.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># TWRP Configuration</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_CUSTOM_CPU_TEMP_PATH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-sr">/sys/devices</span>/<span class="pl-en">virtual</span>/<span class="pl-en">thermal</span>/<span class="pl-en">thermal_zoneNN</span>/<span class="pl-en">temp</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>TW_CUSTOM_CPU_TEMP_PATH ➡️ You should be careful with this, as the Custom Recovery source code was recently updated to automatically search for the path. So you can remove or just put <code>#</code> in front of the path to know that it depends on the addressing, as the <code>thermal_zoneNN</code> (NN is a number) folder is managed on the device.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Modules & Sensors</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_LOAD_VENDOR_MODULES</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s">"aw862xx_haptic.ko"</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_SUPPORT_INPUT_AIDL_HAPTICS</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
<span class="pl-c1">TW_SUPPORT_INPUT_AIDL_HAPTICS_FQNAME</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s">"IVibrator/default"</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>TW_LOAD_VENDOR_MODULES ➡️ The Android <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/kernel/loadable-kernel-modules#android-build-system-support" rel="nofollow">source code</a> already has others variables so that it can compile module <code>*.ko</code> files inside <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/kernel/modules#gki-modules" rel="nofollow">boot.img</a>, recovery.img, <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/kernel/modules#vendor-modules" rel="nofollow">vendor.img</a>, vendor_boot.img and or <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/vendor-odm-dlkm-partition#vendor-contents" rel="nofollow">vendor_dlkm.img</a>. But we have the module files <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/vendor-odm-dlkm-partition#copying-kernels" rel="nofollow">located</a> in \vendor_boot\lib\modules` and so the <code>TW_</code> flag only directs the path to use one or more module files. It appears that the Custom Recovery source code has some attempt to target this automatically, since we all know the location. In this sense, for Android 12+, the modules <code>*.ko</code> files are located inside the <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/vendor-odm-dlkm-partition#vendor-contents" rel="nofollow">vendor_dlkm.img</a> file.</li>
<li>TW_SUPPORT_INPUT_AIDL_HAPTICS ➡️ Set <code>true</code> to enable makefile building for certain hardware.</li>
<li>TW_SUPPORT_INPUT_AIDL_HAPTICS_FQNAME ➡️ Enter the correct path for a given hardware and this will compile the necessary files. In that's case, files for haptics vibrators.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Depending of hardware type the option can have support in AIDL (hal format=" aidl ") mode  or support in HIDL mode (hal format="hidl").
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Example: <code>TW_SUPPORT_INPUT_1_2_HAPTICS := true</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Perhaps Custom Recovery's source code may have updated to find automatically the vibration support. But I haven't really confirmed that. Anyway, personally I don't see this part as a priority and if there is no solution there is the <code>TW_NO_HAPTICS := true</code> flag to disable the vibration.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<section data-footnotes="" class="footnotes"><h2 id="footnote-label" class="sr-only" dir="auto">Footnotes</h2>
<ol dir="auto">
<li id="user-content-fn-%C2%B9-96c048dd4f1699d44cfb72f80bdf1597">
<p dir="auto">Generally the \cache partition is inserted in the mountpoint filesystem in many cases related to the recovery.img file. But it's just a partition like any other and there are no dependencies. It can be relinked with other names like <code>*\by-name\rescue</code>. <a href="#user-content-fnref-%C2%B9-96c048dd4f1699d44cfb72f80bdf1597" data-footnote-backref="" aria-label="Back to reference 1" class="data-footnote-backref">↩</a></p>
</li>
<li id="user-content-fn-%C2%B2-96c048dd4f1699d44cfb72f80bdf1597">
<p dir="auto">But there are some special cases that depend on the kernel version with parts of a legacy device. It is something that depends on the choice of the company that builds the partition structure - mount point and in this case Custom Recovery will only work if you insert the dtbo.img file that is in the firmware file. <a href="#user-content-fnref-%C2%B2-96c048dd4f1699d44cfb72f80bdf1597" data-footnote-backref="" aria-label="Back to reference 2" class="data-footnote-backref">↩</a></p>
</li>
<li id="user-content-fn-%C2%B3-96c048dd4f1699d44cfb72f80bdf1597">
<p dir="auto">The kernel is located only in boot.img which will be called <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/generic-boot">Generic Boot Partition</a>. This entire structure with vendor_boot and Header v4 is called <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/kernel/generic-kernel-image">Generic Kernel Image (GKI)</a>. <a href="#user-content-fnref-%C2%B3-96c048dd4f1699d44cfb72f80bdf1597" data-footnote-backref="" aria-label="Back to reference 3" class="data-footnote-backref">↩</a></p>
</li>
</ol>
</section>
</article>
  </div>

  </div>

</div>
