      <div id="file-4-device-mk_-md-readme" class="Box-body readme blob tmp-p-5 tmp-p-xl-6 gist-border-0" style="overflow: auto" tabindex="0" role="region" aria-label="4. device.mk_.md content, created by lopestom on 02:09AM on January 24, 2024.">
    <article class="markdown-body entry-content container-lg" itemprop="text"><div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h5 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2.2.2.2 The *.mk files</h5><a id="user-content-2222-the-mk-files" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2.2.2.2 The *.mk files" href="#2222-the-mk-files"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h5 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2️⃣ device.mk</h5><a id="user-content-two-devicemk" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: :two: device.mk" href="#two-devicemk"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">For ramdisk recovery with <code>recovery.img</code> file, it is generally not necessary to have <em>device.mk</em> file. But for convenience and custom with the new way of having the device tree, we just reference something simple.</p>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c1">LOCAL_PATH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">device</span>/<span class="pl-en">fplus</span>/<span class="pl-c1">H166</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://developer.android.com/ndk/guides/android_mk#basics" rel="nofollow">LOCAL_PATH</a>  ➡️ This variable indicates the <strong>location of the source files</strong> in the development tree. Here, the macro function my-dir, provided by the build system, returns the <strong>path</strong> of the <strong>current directory</strong> (the directory containing the Android.mk file itself). Note that it is the same path as <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#one-boardconfigmk">DEVICE_PATH</a>.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Dynamic Partitions stuff</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_USE_DYNAMIC_PARTITIONS</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/ota/dynamic_partitions/implement#device-configuration-changes" rel="nofollow">PRODUCT_USE_DYNAMIC_PARTITIONS</a> ➡️ To enable <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/bootloader/fastbootd#dynamic-partitions" rel="nofollow">dynamic partitioning</a> on new devices launching with Android 10 and higher..</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># VNDK</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_TARGET_VNDK_VERSION</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">30</span>

<span class="pl-c"># API</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_SHIPPING_API_LEVEL</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">30</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/vndk#why-vndk" rel="nofollow">PRODUCT_TARGET_VNDK_VERSION</a> ➡️ The VNDK requires several changes to a codebase to separate concerns between vendor and system. The binaries files in each partition must be able to work with each other.</li>
<li><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/security/features/encryption/metadata#switching-on-metadata-encryption" rel="nofollow">PRODUCT_SHIPPING_API_LEVEL</a> ➡️ Since the kernel interface has changed, you need to ensure you set the correct value for this and it must contain the correct <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/aidl/stable-aidl#cuttlefish-dev-tool" rel="nofollow">Android version</a> if your device starts with Android 11+.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Some SDK/API informations: <a href="https://apilevels.com/" rel="nofollow">Android API Levels</a></li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_PLATFORM</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">mt6765</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_BOARD</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">k62v1_64_h5</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>PRODUCT_PLATFORM ➡️ To define a hardware platform that configures the processes and routes necessary to assemble a specific Product-Chipset. Copy the same value from <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#one-boardconfigmk">TARGET_BOARD_PLATFORM</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/setup/create/new-device#build-layers" rel="nofollow">PRODUCT_BOARD</a> ➡️ Same idea as above. Copy the same value from TARGET_BOOTLOADER_BOARD_NAME.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Fastbootd stuff</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_PACKAGES</span> += \
    <span class="pl-en">android</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">hardware</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">fastboot</span>@<span class="pl-c1">1.0</span>-<span class="pl-en">impl</span>-<span class="pl-en">mock</span> \
    <span class="pl-en">android</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">hardware</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">fastboot</span>@<span class="pl-c1">1.0</span>-<span class="pl-en">impl</span>-<span class="pl-en">mock</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">recovery</span> \
    <span class="pl-en">fastbootd</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/setup/create/new-device#prod-def" rel="nofollow">PRODUCT_PACKAGES</a> ➡️ List of the modules to build. In this case, the device needs <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/ota/dynamic_partitions/implement#fastbootd" rel="nofollow">fastbootd</a> binary files.</li>
<li><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/bootloader/fastbootd" rel="nofollow">fastbootd</a> ➡️ An optional module is installed only if it's required by the product configuration with PRODUCT_PACKAGES. The Custom Recovery need the <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/bootloader/fastbootd#unifying" rel="nofollow">fastboot & fastbootd</a> for manage and flash files in the super.img partition.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># health Hal</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_PACKAGES</span> += \
    <span class="pl-en">android</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">hardware</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">health</span>@<span class="pl-c1">2.1</span>-<span class="pl-en">impl</span> \
    <span class="pl-en">android</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">hardware</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">health</span>@<span class="pl-c1">2.1</span>-<span class="pl-en">service</span> \</pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/perf/health" rel="nofollow">health</a> ➡️ The files are compiled to manage the health part. There are <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/perf/health#requirements" rel="nofollow">requirements</a> depending on each Android version.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h5 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2️⃣ device.mk for Recovery_ramdisk in the boot.img</h5><a id="user-content-two-devicemk-for-recovery_ramdisk-in-the-bootimg" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: :two: device.mk for Recovery_ramdisk in the boot.img" href="#two-devicemk-for-recovery_ramdisk-in-the-bootimg"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre> <span class="pl-c"># VIRTUAL A/B</span>
<span class="pl-c1">ENABLE_VIRTUAL_AB</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
 
<span class="pl-c"># A/B</span>
<span class="pl-c1">AB_OTA_UPDATER</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">true</span>

<span class="pl-c1">AB_OTA_PARTITIONS</span> += \
    <span class="pl-en">system</span> \
    <span class="pl-en">vendor</span> \
    <span class="pl-en">product</span> \
    <span class="pl-en">boot</span> \
    <span class="pl-en">vbmeta_vendor</span> \
    <span class="pl-en">vbmeta_system</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">This part is connected with <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#three-twrp_devicenamemk-for-recovery_ramdisk-in-the-bootimg"><code># Virtual A/B OTA</code></a> because the <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/ota/ab/ab_implement#recovery" rel="nofollow">recovery ramdisk</a> is in the boot.img file. Don't expect any Custom Recovery to install OEM <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/ota/ab" rel="nofollow">update files</a>.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>ENABLE_VIRTUAL_AB ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by <code>ro.virtual_ab.enabled=true</code> line. This variable <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/ota/ab/ab_implement" rel="nofollow">Implement A/B updates</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/ota/ab/ab_implement#build-variables" rel="nofollow">AB_OTA_UPDATER</a> ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by <code>ro.build.ab_update=true</code> line. Set <code>true</code> to enable updating partitions through update_engine.</li>
<li><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/ota/ab/ab_implement#configuration" rel="nofollow">AB_OTA_PARTITIONS</a> ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by <code>ro.product.ab_ota_partitions=boot,system,vendor,product</code> line. Configured by the the payload generator defines an update for list of any pair of <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/ota/ab/ab_implement#partitions" rel="nofollow">A/B partitions</a> defined in the same disk.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c1">AB_OTA_POSTINSTALL_CONFIG</span> += \
    <span class="pl-v">RUN_POSTINSTALL_system</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">true</span> \
    <span class="pl-v">POSTINSTALL_PATH_system</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-en">system</span>/<span class="pl-en">bin</span>/<span class="pl-en">otapreopt_script</span> \
    <span class="pl-v">FILESYSTEM_TYPE_system</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-en">ext4</span> \
    <span class="pl-v">POSTINSTALL_OPTIONAL_system</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">true</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/ota/ab/ab_implement#compilation" rel="nofollow">AB_OTA_POSTINSTALL_CONFIG</a> ➡️ It is determined by the AOSP source code.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Boot control HAL</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_PACKAGES</span> += \
    <span class="pl-en">android</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">hardware</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">boot</span>@<span class="pl-c1">1.1</span>-<span class="pl-en">impl</span>-<span class="pl-en">recovery</span> \
    <span class="pl-en">android</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">hardware</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">boot</span>@<span class="pl-c1">1.1</span>-<span class="pl-en">impl</span> \
    <span class="pl-en">android</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">hardware</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">boot</span>@<span class="pl-c1">1.1</span>-<span class="pl-en">service</span>
    <span class="pl-en">bootctrl</span>

<span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_PACKAGES</span> += \
    <span class="pl-en">bootctrl</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span>$<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">TARGET_BOARD_PLATFORM</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span> \
    <span class="pl-en">bootctrl</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span>$<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">TARGET_BOARD_PLATFORM</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">recovery</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/setup/create/new-device#prod-def" rel="nofollow">PRODUCT_PACKAGES</a> ➡️ List of the modules to build. In this case, the device needs <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/security/features/verifiedboot/boot-flow#flow-for-a-b-devices" rel="nofollow">bootctrl</a> or <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/ota/virtual_ab/implement#boot-control-hal" rel="nofollow">snapuserd</a> and some <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/ota/ab/ab_implement#bootcontrol" rel="nofollow">Boot control</a> binary files.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_PACKAGES_DEBUG</span> += \
    <span class="pl-en">bootctrl</span> \
    <span class="pl-en">update_engine_client</span>

<span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_PACKAGES</span> += \
    <span class="pl-en">otapreopt_script</span> \
    <span class="pl-en">update_engine</span> \
    <span class="pl-en">update_verifier</span> \
    <span class="pl-en">update_engine_sideload</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/ota/ab/ab_implement#build-variables" rel="nofollow">PRODUCT_PACKAGES_DEBUG</a> ➡️ Optional for debug builds.</li>
<li>PRODUCT_PACKAGES ➡️ List of the modules to build. In this case, the update process needs some files to manage process.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># MTK PlPath Utils</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_PACKAGES</span> += \
    <span class="pl-en">mtk_plpath_utils</span> \
    <span class="pl-en">mtk_plpath_utils</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">recovery</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>PRODUCT_PACKAGES ➡️ List of the modules to build. In this case, a set of path-related utility that manage MTK storage.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<p dir="auto">If your device has A12+ with <strong>emulated storage without sdcardfs</strong>, add the same variable as vendor_boot is defined.</p>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<hr>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h5 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2️⃣ device.mk for Recovery_ramdisk in the vendor_boot.img</h5><a id="user-content-two-devicemk-for-recovery_ramdisk-in-the-vendor_bootimg" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: :two: device.mk for Recovery_ramdisk in the vendor_boot.img" href="#two-devicemk-for-recovery_ramdisk-in-the-vendor_bootimg"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">For ramdisk recovery with <code>vendor_boot.img</code> file, the boot.img file is called <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/partitions/generic-boot" rel="nofollow">Generic Boot Partition</a> because contains the generic ramdisk and the GKI kernel.</p>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Enable project quotas and casefolding for emulated storage without sdcardfs - </span>
<span class="pl-c"># SDCard replacement functionality</span>
$<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-en">call</span> <span class="pl-en">inherit</span>-<span class="pl-en">product</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">SRC_TARGET_DIR</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">product</span>/<span class="pl-en">emulated_storage</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">mk</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform/build/+/HEAD/target/product/emulated_storage.mk" rel="nofollow">emulated_storage.mk</a> ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by <code>external_storage.projid.enabled=1</code>, <code>external_storage.casefold.enabled=1</code> and <code>external_storage.sdcardfs.enabled=0</code> lines that enable <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/storage/fuse-passthrough" rel="nofollow">FUSE passthrough</a> with A12+ and update <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/storage/sdcardfs-deprecate" rel="nofollow">Emulate Storage</a>.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
</article>
  </div>

  </div>

</div>

      <div id="file-5-twrp_devicename-mk_-md-readme" class="Box-body readme blob tmp-p-5 tmp-p-xl-6 gist-border-0" style="overflow: auto" tabindex="0" role="region" aria-label="5. twrp_devicename.mk_.md content, created by lopestom on 02:09AM on January 24, 2024.">
    <article class="markdown-body entry-content container-lg" itemprop="text"><div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h5 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2.2.2.3 The *.mk files</h5><a id="user-content-2223-the-mk-files" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2.2.2.3 The *.mk files" href="#2223-the-mk-files"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h5 class="heading-element" dir="auto">3️⃣ twrp_devicename.mk</h5><a id="user-content-three-twrp_devicenamemk" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: :three: twrp_devicename.mk" href="#three-twrp_devicenamemk"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Inherit from those products. Most specific first.</span>
$<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-en">call</span> <span class="pl-en">inherit</span>-<span class="pl-en">product</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">SRC_TARGET_DIR</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">product</span>/<span class="pl-en">core_64_bit</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">mk</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
$<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-en">call</span> <span class="pl-en">inherit</span>-<span class="pl-en">product</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">SRC_TARGET_DIR</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">product</span>/<span class="pl-en">aosp_base</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">mk</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-c">#$(call inherit-product, $(SRC_TARGET_DIR)/product/base.mk)</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform/build/+/master/target/product/core_64_bit.mk" rel="nofollow">core_64_bit.mk</a> ➡️ It is determined by the AOSP source code. Always use for 64-bit devices, to compile binary files and leave the structure in accordance with the <code>Architecture</code>.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>For <a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform/build/+/08d410f53249c18d752f56a881ed2335403080d4/target/product/core_minimal.mk" rel="nofollow">32-bit</a> devices, remove the above 64-bit part and use:
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre>   <span class="pl-c"># Inherit from those products. Most specific first.</span>
   $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-en">call</span> <span class="pl-en">inherit</span>-<span class="pl-en">product</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">SRC_TARGET_DIR</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">product</span>/<span class="pl-en">core_minimal</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">mk</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span></pre></div>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform/build/+/lollipop-release/target/product/aosp_base.mk" rel="nofollow">aosp_base.mk</a> ➡️ It is determined by the AOSP source code. You can use this or <a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform/build/+/lollipop-release/target/product/base.mk" rel="nofollow"><code>base.mk</code></a> if you want.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Inherit from star device</span>
$<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-en">call</span> <span class="pl-en">inherit</span>-<span class="pl-en">product</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-en">device</span>/<span class="pl-en">brand</span>/<span class="pl-en">devicename</span>/<span class="pl-en">device</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">mk</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>device.mk ➡️ Comes from android_device_brand_devicename/device.mk file. This call all variables and flags in the device.mk file to build all necesssary.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Inherit some common recovery stuff.</span>
$<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-en">call</span> <span class="pl-en">inherit</span>-<span class="pl-en">product</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">twrp</span>/<span class="pl-en">config</span>/<span class="pl-en">common</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">mk</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>common.mk ➡️ Comes from <a href="https://github.com/TeamWin/android_vendor_twrp/blob/android-11/config/common.mk">Custom Recovery source code</a>.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Device identifier. This must come after all inclusions</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_DEVICE</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">H166</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_NAME</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">twrp_H166</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_BRAND</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-v">Fplus</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_MODEL</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">H166</span>
<span class="pl-c1">PRODUCT_MANUFACTURER</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-v">Fplus</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>PRODUCT_DEVICE ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by any <code>*.device=</code>.</li>
<li>PRODUCT_NAME ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by any <code>*.device=</code>.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>It should actually come from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by any <code>*.name=</code>. But by Custom Recovery or <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/setup/create/new-device#makefiles" rel="nofollow">AOSP source code</a> rules, it must be the same as PRODUCT_DEVICE after <code>twrp_</code>.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>PRODUCT_BRAND ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by any <code>*.brand=</code>.</li>
<li>PRODUCT_MODEL ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by any <code>*.model=</code>.</li>
<li>PRODUCT_MANUFACTURER ➡️ Comes from <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file by any <code>*.manufacture=</code>.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Some of the variables maintained in a <code>PRODUCT_</code> definition file have a <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/setup/create/new-device#prod-def" rel="nofollow">table</a>.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h5 class="heading-element" dir="auto">3️⃣ twrp_devicename.mk for Recovery_ramdisk in the boot.img</h5><a id="user-content-three-twrp_devicenamemk-for-recovery_ramdisk-in-the-bootimg" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: :three: twrp_devicename.mk for Recovery_ramdisk in the boot.img" href="#three-twrp_devicenamemk-for-recovery_ramdisk-in-the-bootimg"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Installs gsi keys into ramdisk, to boot a developer GSI with verified boot.</span>
$<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-en">call</span> <span class="pl-en">inherit</span>-<span class="pl-en">product</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">SRC_TARGET_DIR</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">product</span>/<span class="pl-en">gsi_keys</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">mk</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform/build/+/HEAD/target/product/developer_gsi_keys.mk" rel="nofollow">gsi_keys.mk</a> ➡️ It is determined by the AOSP source code. Installs gsi keys into ramdisk, to boot a developer GSI with verified boot.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Virtual A/B OTA</span>
$<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-en">call</span> <span class="pl-en">inherit</span>-<span class="pl-en">product</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">SRC_TARGET_DIR</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">product</span>/<span class="pl-en">virtual_ab_ota</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">mk</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform/build/+/refs/heads/android11-release/target/product/virtual_ab_ota.mk" rel="nofollow">virtual_ab_ota.mk</a> ➡️ It is determined by the AOSP source code. It is determined by the AOSP source code. Install some files in <code>\system\bin</code> and <code>\system\lib64</code> to correctly start updating the A/B structure and thus the OTA files.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Look, this mode is with boot_ramdisk and therefore, many files and processes change, specially in the device.mk file because of the Android11+ structure, so they need to be updated and redone. This line can inside in the device.mk file and if you choose that, so remove line from twrp_devicename.mk file.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<hr>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h5 class="heading-element" dir="auto">3️⃣ twrp_devicename.mk for Recovery_ramdisk in the vendor_boot.img</h5><a id="user-content-three-twrp_devicenamemk-for-recovery_ramdisk-in-the-vendor_bootimg" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: :three: twrp_devicename.mk for Recovery_ramdisk in the vendor_boot.img" href="#three-twrp_devicenamemk-for-recovery_ramdisk-in-the-vendor_bootimg"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"># Configure launch_with_vendor_ramdisk.mk</span>
$<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-en">call</span> <span class="pl-en">inherit</span>-<span class="pl-en">product</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">SRC_TARGET_DIR</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>/<span class="pl-en">product</span>/<span class="pl-en">virtual_ab_ota</span>/<span class="pl-en">launch_with_vendor_ramdisk</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">mk</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform/build/+/refs/heads/android12-dev/target/product/virtual_ab_ota/launch_with_vendor_ramdisk.mk" rel="nofollow">launch_with_vendor_ramdisk.mk</a> ➡️ It is determined by the AOSP source code. Devices launching with <code>Virtual A/B</code> and has a <code>vendor_boot</code> partition is preferred to inherit from this makefile.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Look, this mode is with vendor_boot_ramdisk and therefore, many files and processes change, specially in the device.mk file because of the Android12+ structure, so they need to be updated and redone. This line can inside in the device.mk file and if you choose that, so remove line from twrp_devicename.mk file.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<hr>
 <p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-note" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg class="octicon octicon-info mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M0 8a8 8 0 1 1 16 0A8 8 0 0 1 0 8Zm8-6.5a6.5 6.5 0 1 0 0 13 6.5 6.5 0 0 0 0-13ZM6.5 7.75A.75.75 0 0 1 7.25 7h1a.75.75 0 0 1 .75.75v2.75h.25a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-2a.75.75 0 0 1 0-1.5h.25v-2h-.25a.75.75 0 0 1-.75-.75ZM8 6a1 1 0 1 1 0-2 1 1 0 0 1 0 2Z"></path></svg>Note</p><p dir="auto">Understand that you must change some parts and know the difference to compile Custom Recovery with <code>omni_</code> for A9 and earlier versions, as well as with <code>twrp_</code> for A10 and later versions.
For A9 and earlier use the minimal manifest <a href="https://github.com/minimal-manifest-twrp/platform_manifest_twrp_omni">platform_manifest_twrp_omni</a>. I think you understand why there is <code>omni</code> at the end of the word.
For A10 and later use the minimal manifest <a href="https://github.com/minimal-manifest-twrp/platform_manifest_twrp_aosp">platform_manifest_twrp_aosp</a>.</p>
</div>
</article>

<div class="js-gist-file-update-container js-task-list-container">
      <div id="file-6-android-mk-androidproducts-mk_-md" class="file my-2">

    <article class="markdown-body entry-content container-lg" itemprop="text"><div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h5 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2.2.2.4 The *.mk files</h5><a id="user-content-2224-the-mk-files" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2.2.2.4 The *.mk files" href="#2224-the-mk-files"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c1">LOCAL_PATH</span> :<span class="pl-c1">=</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-en">call</span> <span class="pl-en">my</span>-<span class="pl-en">dir</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>

<span class="pl-en">ifeq</span> <span class="pl-kos">(</span>$<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">TARGET_DEVICE</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-c1">H166</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-en">include</span> $<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-en">call</span> <span class="pl-en">all</span>-<span class="pl-en">subdir</span>-<span class="pl-en">makefiles</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>$<span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">LOCAL_PATH</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>

      <div id="file-7-recovery_root-md-readme" class="Box-body readme blob tmp-p-5 tmp-p-xl-6 gist-border-0" style="overflow: auto" tabindex="0" role="region" aria-label="7. recovery_root.md content, created by lopestom on 02:09AM on January 24, 2024.">
    <article class="markdown-body entry-content container-lg" itemprop="text"><div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-caution" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg class="octicon octicon-stop mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M4.47.22A.749.749 0 0 1 5 0h6c.199 0 .389.079.53.22l4.25 4.25c.141.14.22.331.22.53v6a.749.749 0 0 1-.22.53l-4.25 4.25A.749.749 0 0 1 11 16H5a.749.749 0 0 1-.53-.22L.22 11.53A.749.749 0 0 1 0 11V5c0-.199.079-.389.22-.53Zm.84 1.28L1.5 5.31v5.38l3.81 3.81h5.38l3.81-3.81V5.31L10.69 1.5ZM8 4a.75.75 0 0 1 .75.75v3.5a.75.75 0 0 1-1.5 0v-3.5A.75.75 0 0 1 8 4Zm0 8a1 1 0 1 1 0-2 1 1 0 0 1 0 2Z"></path></svg>Caution</p><p dir="auto">There are many different files that depend on what the device can have. You can read more about these files in the InfoGuide <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/3c1f3eaa66248c56e61acf19ddd4b96c#understanding-if-the-custom-recovery-device-tree-for-mediatek-devices-is-good-for-compiling">recovery</a>.
Therefore, the approach below only serves as an information base for a given device, but obviously it can have many characteristics that are the same or different from other devices.</p>
</div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2.3 The folders /recovery/root/</h3><a id="user-content-23-the-folders-recoveryroot" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2.3 The folders /recovery/root/" href="#23-the-folders-recoveryroot"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/25868008/299788685-a07b7190-404d-4d8a-8dbc-78e1298f4c85.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..oALD9GKQFTnG9kLLPJD3l18ULhiWvlv_-jz7G7VwAdo"><img src="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/25868008/299788685-a07b7190-404d-4d8a-8dbc-78e1298f4c85.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..oALD9GKQFTnG9kLLPJD3l18ULhiWvlv_-jz7G7VwAdo" alt="ksnip_20240125-160848" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<p dir="auto">The Device Tree has these folders containing files that will be inserted directly into <code>ramdisk</code>. The structure of this part is the same as any stock ramdisk recovery and therefore must also follow the structure of the img stock file.
In this mode we can copy the folders with the files we need and want directly to the <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#file-9-finishing-md">device tree</a>. So pay close attention to the img stock file and its folders and files so that anything that needs to be copied is actually inserted into the Device Tree.</p>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-important" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg class="octicon octicon-report mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M0 1.75C0 .784.784 0 1.75 0h12.5C15.216 0 16 .784 16 1.75v9.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.25 13H8.06l-2.573 2.573A1.458 1.458 0 0 1 3 14.543V13H1.75A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 0 11.25Zm1.75-.25a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v9.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h2a.75.75 0 0 1 .75.75v2.19l2.72-2.72a.749.749 0 0 1 .53-.22h6.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-9.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25-.25Zm7 2.25v2.5a.75.75 0 0 1-1.5 0v-2.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0ZM9 9a1 1 0 1 1-2 0 1 1 0 0 1 2 0Z"></path></svg>Important</p><p dir="auto">By way of understanding, this part does not involve the inclusion of encryption/decryption files. This part is in item <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#3-crypt-decrypt">3. Crypt-Decrypt</a> and if you want a device tree with that part so can put everything together in one if that is your choice.</p>
</div>
<p dir="auto">We start by understanding the need to check the folder and file structure of stock the recovery ramdisk so that if there are any folders and files to be included in the device tree, we should do that. But in most situations, we only interact with a maximum of 5 folders with files and some specific files directly in the <strong>\recovery\<code>root\</code></strong> folder. Don't be surprised if there is no folder or file on your stock recovery ramdisk like in the example below. Just copy what you need according to what exists on your recovery ramdisk!</p>
<p dir="auto">In the root folder the device tree can have the correct file(s) mostly as the init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc; init.recovery.usb.rc and other init.*.rc or *.rc files for encrypt-decrypt process.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2.3.1 \recovery\root\</h4><a id="user-content-231-recoveryroot" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2.3.1 \recovery\root\" href="#231-recoveryroot"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><code>init.recovery.usb.rc</code></p>
<p dir="auto">Inside this file, there are lines of programming that refer to information that might be important to start the addressing part of USB-related drivers like adb; fastboot; MTP; fastbootd and others you need. This file also contains permission lines, the references (letters and numbers) that will direct so that Custom Recovery can have access to the internal and external storage, in addition to being able to be seen by a file manager of a laptop, computer or even another smartphone. So the device with Custom Recovery can use too platform-tools (adb-fastboot-fastbootd) to access informations, folders, files, flash files, copying anything, etc.</p>
<p dir="auto">There are many examples for many Mediatek devices. For this reason, the following example can be used, but you should be aware that it may not be good for your Chipset device. You should compare the examples with the stock file located at <strong>\vendor\etc\init\hw\<code>init.mtNNNN.usb.rc</code></strong> for improvements and understanding.</p>
<p dir="auto">Carry out tests and find the best file that can solve the case.</p>
   <details><summary>Example - Click to open</summary>
   <p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre>  <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">early</span>-<span class="pl-en">init</span>
      <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/sys/module</span>/<span class="pl-en">musb_hdrc</span>/<span class="pl-en">parameters</span>/<span class="pl-en">kernel_init_done</span> <span class="pl-c1">1</span>

  <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">init</span>
      <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">sys</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">usb</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">configfs</span> <span class="pl-c1">1</span>
      <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">sys</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">usb</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">ffs</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">aio_compat</span> <span class="pl-c1">1</span>
      <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">usb</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">controller</span> <span class="pl-s">"musb-hdrc"</span>
      <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">usb</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">acm_cnt</span> <span class="pl-c1">0</span>

  <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-pds">property</span>:<span class="pl-en">sys</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">usb</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">ffs</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">ready</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span> && <span class="pl-en">property</span>:<span class="pl-en">sys</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">usb</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">config</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-en">mtp</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-en">adb</span> && <span class="pl-en">property</span>:<span class="pl-en">sys</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">usb</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">configfs</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span>
      <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">sys</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">usb</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">ffs</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">mtp</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">ready</span> <span class="pl-c1">1</span>
      <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/config/usb</span><span class="pl-en">_gadget</span>/<span class="pl-en">g1</span>/<span class="pl-en">configs</span>/<span class="pl-en">b</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span>/<span class="pl-en">strings</span>/<span class="pl-c1">0x409</span>/<span class="pl-en">configuration</span> <span class="pl-s">"mtp_adb"</span>
      <span class="pl-en">rm</span> <span class="pl-sr">/config/usb</span><span class="pl-en">_gadget</span>/<span class="pl-en">g1</span>/<span class="pl-en">configs</span>/<span class="pl-en">b</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span>/<span class="pl-en">f1</span>
      <span class="pl-en">rm</span> <span class="pl-sr">/config/usb</span><span class="pl-en">_gadget</span>/<span class="pl-en">g1</span>/<span class="pl-en">configs</span>/<span class="pl-en">b</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span>/<span class="pl-en">f2</span>
      <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/config/usb</span><span class="pl-en">_gadget</span>/<span class="pl-en">g1</span>/<span class="pl-en">idVendor</span> <span class="pl-c1">0x22d9</span>
      <span class="pl-en">write</span> /<span class="pl-en">config</span>/<span class="pl-en">usb_gadget</span>/<span class="pl-en">g1</span>/<span class="pl-en">idProduct</span> <span class="pl-c1">0x2765</span>
      <span class="pl-en">write</span> /<span class="pl-en">config</span>/<span class="pl-en">usb_gadget</span>/<span class="pl-en">g1</span>/<span class="pl-en">os_desc</span>/<span class="pl-en">use</span> <span class="pl-c1">1</span>
      <span class="pl-en">symlink</span> /<span class="pl-en">config</span>/<span class="pl-en">usb_gadget</span>/<span class="pl-en">g1</span>/<span class="pl-en">functions</span>/<span class="pl-en">mtp</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">gs0</span> /<span class="pl-en">config</span>/<span class="pl-en">usb_gadget</span>/<span class="pl-en">g1</span>/<span class="pl-en">configs</span>/<span class="pl-en">b</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span>/<span class="pl-en">f1</span>
      <span class="pl-en">symlink</span> <span class="pl-sr">/config/usb</span><span class="pl-en">_gadget</span>/<span class="pl-en">g1</span>/<span class="pl-en">functions</span>/<span class="pl-en">ffs</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">adb</span> /<span class="pl-en">config</span>/<span class="pl-en">usb_gadget</span>/<span class="pl-en">g1</span>/<span class="pl-en">configs</span>/<span class="pl-en">b</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span>/<span class="pl-en">f2</span>
      <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/config/usb</span><span class="pl-en">_gadget</span>/<span class="pl-en">g1</span>/<span class="pl-c1">UDC</span> $<span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-en">sys</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">usb</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">controller</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span>
      <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">sys</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">usb</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">state</span> $<span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-en">sys</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">usb</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">config</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span></pre></div>
   <p dir="auto"></p>
   </details>
<p dir="auto">For knowledge, you can read and try something with the guide <a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform/bootable/recovery/+/refs/heads/android13-platform-release#troubleshooting" rel="nofollow">Troubleshooting</a> or/and if device is using configfs, check <a href="https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/usb/gadget_configfs.txt" rel="nofollow">Linux USB gadget</a> instructions.</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</code></p>
<p dir="auto">Comes from stock ramdisk_recovery by init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc file. For Custom Recovery builds without encryption/decryption, we just copy the stock init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc file and add some small extra parts.</p>
   <details><summary>Example - Click to open</summary>
   <p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre>   <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">init</span>
       <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">sys</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">usb</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">configfs</span> <span class="pl-c1">1</span>
       <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">sys</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">usb</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">ffs</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">aio_compat</span> <span class="pl-c1">0</span>

   <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">fs</span> && <span class="pl-en">property</span>:<span class="pl-en">ro</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">debuggable</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">0</span>
       <span class="pl-c"># distinguish USB shoulde connect or not, i.e. CDP vs SDP</span>
       <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/sys/class</span>/<span class="pl-en">udc</span>/<span class="pl-en">musb</span>-<span class="pl-en">hdrc</span>/<span class="pl-en">device</span>/<span class="pl-en">cmode</span> <span class="pl-c1">2</span>
       <span class="pl-c"># set charging free due to it wait for USB activation</span>
       <span class="pl-en">start</span> <span class="pl-en">adbd</span>

   <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">post</span>-<span class="pl-en">fs</span>
       <span class="pl-c"># Support A/B feature for EMMC and UFS boot region</span>
       <span class="pl-en">symlink</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/block</span>/<span class="pl-en">sda</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">mmcblk0boot0</span>
       <span class="pl-en">symlink</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/block</span>/<span class="pl-en">sdb</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">mmcblk0boot1</span>
       <span class="pl-en">symlink</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/block</span>/<span class="pl-en">mmcblk0boot0</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">platform</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">preloader_a</span>
       <span class="pl-en">symlink</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/block</span>/<span class="pl-en">mmcblk0boot1</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">platform</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">preloader_b</span>
       <span class="pl-en">symlink</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/block</span>/<span class="pl-en">mmcblk0boot0</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">preloader_a</span>
       <span class="pl-en">symlink</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/block</span>/<span class="pl-en">mmcblk0boot1</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">preloader_b</span>

       <span class="pl-en">exec</span> <span class="pl-pds">u</span>:<span class="pl-en">r</span>:<span class="pl-pds">update_engine</span>:<span class="pl-en">s0</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span> -- <span class="pl-sr">/system/bin</span>/<span class="pl-en">mtk_plpath_utils</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">The above part should come from your stock init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc file. The part below has extra inclusions. Combine the two parts into a single init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc file.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre>       <span class="pl-en">start</span> <span class="pl-en">mtk</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">plpath</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">utils</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">link</span>

   <span class="pl-en">service</span> <span class="pl-en">mtk</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">plpath</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">utils</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">link</span> <span class="pl-sr">/system/bin</span>/<span class="pl-en">mtk_plpath_utils</span>
       <span class="pl-k">class</span> <span class="pl-en">main</span>
       <span class="pl-en">user</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span>
       <span class="pl-en">group</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span>
       <span class="pl-en">oneshot</span>
       <span class="pl-en">disabled</span>
       <span class="pl-en">seclabel</span> <span class="pl-pds">u</span><span class="pl-pds">:r</span><span class="pl-pds">:recovery</span><span class="pl-pds">:s0</span>

   <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">boot</span>
        <span class="pl-en">start</span> <span class="pl-en">boot</span>-<span class="pl-en">hal</span>-<span class="pl-c1">1</span>-<span class="pl-c1">2</span>
        <span class="pl-en">start</span> <span class="pl-en">health</span>-<span class="pl-en">hal</span>-<span class="pl-c1">2</span>-<span class="pl-c1">1</span></pre></div>
   <p dir="auto"></p>
   </details>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-tip" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg class="octicon octicon-light-bulb mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M8 1.5c-2.363 0-4 1.69-4 3.75 0 .984.424 1.625.984 2.304l.214.253c.223.264.47.556.673.848.284.411.537.896.621 1.49a.75.75 0 0 1-1.484.211c-.04-.282-.163-.547-.37-.847a8.456 8.456 0 0 0-.542-.68c-.084-.1-.173-.205-.268-.32C3.201 7.75 2.5 6.766 2.5 5.25 2.5 2.31 4.863 0 8 0s5.5 2.31 5.5 5.25c0 1.516-.701 2.5-1.328 3.259-.095.115-.184.22-.268.319-.207.245-.383.453-.541.681-.208.3-.33.565-.37.847a.751.751 0 0 1-1.485-.212c.084-.593.337-1.078.621-1.489.203-.292.45-.584.673-.848.075-.088.147-.173.213-.253.561-.679.985-1.32.985-2.304 0-2.06-1.637-3.75-4-3.75ZM5.75 12h4.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-4.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0-1.5ZM6 15.25a.75.75 0 0 1 .75-.75h2.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-2.5a.75.75 0 0 1-.75-.75Z"></path></svg>Tip</p><p dir="auto">Pay attention to the version of the stock files <code>boot-hal-*N-*n</code>; <code>health-hal-*N-*n</code> as they may be different due to the Android version.</p>
</div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2.3.2 \recovery\root\lib\modules\</h4><a id="user-content-232-recoveryrootlibmodules" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2.3.2 \recovery\root\lib\modules\" href="#232-recoveryrootlibmodules"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">As we know from the InfoGuide <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/685cdd9c71476e9daf95ca612066e23f#it-remained-only-to-find-out-which-file-is-responsible-for-touching-the-screen">Touchscreen not working</a> for Android 12+ version, the <code>*.ko</code> file folder with hardware modules is located in <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/685cdd9c71476e9daf95ca612066e23f#current-conditions">certain folders</a> of the stock recovery_ramdisk and/or also in the stock vendor.img file. These <code>*.ko</code> module files will be useful for mainly enabling touch screen.</p>
<p dir="auto">Either way, <strong>copy the entire stock folder and ALL files</strong>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2.3.3 \recovery\root\first_stage_ramdisk</h4><a id="user-content-233-recoveryrootfirst_stage_ramdisk" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2.3.3 \recovery\root\first_stage_ramdisk" href="#233-recoveryrootfirst_stage_ramdisk"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">As we know by the <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#file-2-mediatek-devices-vs-knowledge-md">partition dependency information Table</a>, the first_stage_ramdisk folder is only needed if your ramdisk_recovery does not have the recovery.img file.</p>
<p dir="auto">Inside these folder has files or a unique file like <code>fstab.mtNNNN</code> or and <code>fstab.emmc</code>. Copy only the file(s) from the same stock recovery_ramdisk\first_stage_ramdisk folder.</p>
<hr>
<p dir="auto">Some folders are mandatory because they contain important files that must be included in the device tree. But depending on each case, device-Chipset and Android version, we may include folders and files in the device tree that do not exist in the stock recovery ramdisk. These new folders and files can be used to try to solve something or just customize, as long as they are for really useful purposes. By adding irrelevant folders and files, you will be adding empty and useless parts to Custom Recovery.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2.3.4 \recovery\root\system\</h4><a id="user-content-234-recoveryrootsystem" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2.3.4 \recovery\root\system\" href="#234-recoveryrootsystem"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto"><code>bin\</code></p>
<p dir="auto"><code>mtk_plpath_utils</code></p>
<p dir="auto">Copy the file from the same stock recovery_ramdisk\bin folder.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto"><code>etc\</code></p>
<p dir="auto"><code>fstab.postinstall</code></p>
<p dir="auto"><code>task_profiles.json</code></p>
<p dir="auto">Copy the files from the same stock system.img in the \system\etc folder.</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>recovery.fstab</code></p>
<p dir="auto">Copy the <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/kernel/mounting-partitions-early#fstab-entries" rel="nofollow">fstab</a> file from the same stock system.img in the \system\etc folder. But for quick and performance mounting important <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/ota/nonab/device_code#partition-map" rel="nofollow">partition map</a>, leave this file with only the some partitions.</p>
 <details><summary>Example - Click to open</summary>
 <p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre>   <span class="pl-c"># 341 "<built-in>" 3</span>
   <span class="pl-c"># 141 "vendor/mediatek/proprietary/hardware/fstab/mt6877/fstab.in.mt6877"</span>
   <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/system ext4 ro wait,slotselect,avb=vbmeta_system,logical,first_stage_mount,avb_keys=/avb</span>/<span class="pl-en">q</span>-<span class="pl-en">gsi</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">avbpubkey</span>:/<span class="pl-en">avb</span>/<span class="pl-en">r</span>-<span class="pl-en">gsi</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">avbpubkey</span>:/<span class="pl-en">avb</span>/<span class="pl-en">s</span>-<span class="pl-en">gsi</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">avbpubkey</span>
   <span class="pl-en">vendor</span> <span class="pl-sr">/vendor ext4 ro wait,slotselect,avb,logical,first_stage_mount</span>
<span class="pl-sr">   product /product</span> <span class="pl-en">ext4</span> <span class="pl-en">ro</span> <span class="pl-en">wait</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-en">slotselect</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-en">avb</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-en">logical</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-en">first_stage_mount</span>
 
   <span class="pl-sr">/dev/block</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">metadata</span>    /<span class="pl-s1">metadata</span>    <span class="pl-en">ext4</span>    <span class="pl-en">noatime</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-s1">nosuid</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-s1">nodev</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-s1">discard</span>    <span class="pl-en">wait</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-s1">check</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-s1">formattable</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-en">first_stage_mount</span>

   <span class="pl-sr">/dev/block</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">userdata</span>    <span class="pl-sr">/data    f2fs    noatime,nosuid,nodev,discard,noflush_merge,fsync_mode=nobarrier,reserve_root=134217,resgid=1065,inlinecrypt wait,check,formattable,quota,reservedsize=128m,latemount,resize,checkpoint=fs,fileencryption=aes-256-xts:aes-256-cts:v2+inlinecrypt_optimized,keydirectory=/metadata</span>/<span class="pl-en">vold</span>/<span class="pl-s1">metadata_encryption</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-en">fsverity</span>

   <span class="pl-sr">/dev/block</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">misc</span>    <span class="pl-sr">/misc    emmc    defaults    defaults</span>
<span class="pl-sr">   /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">boot</span>    /<span class="pl-en">boot</span>    <span class="pl-en">emmc</span>    <span class="pl-en">defaults</span>    <span class="pl-s1">first_stage_mount</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-s1">nofail</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span><span class="pl-s1">slotselect</span></pre></div>
 <p dir="auto"></p>
 </details>
<p dir="auto"><code>twrp.flags</code></p>
<p dir="auto">The twrp.fstab or twrp.flags files must contain the necessary partitions for mounting, backup and also for the Custom Recovery to be able to make the MicroSD block partition (<a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/storage/config#file_mappings" rel="nofollow">external_sd</a>) and or OTG examine and mount (open) this in your internal filemanager or external filemanagers as well as give read and write capability.
The twrp.flags files must be edited according to your stock recovery.fstab file. But as a rule of Custom Recovery, the way it is written will be different and therefore must follow the following sequence:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-yaml" dir="auto"><pre>    <span class="pl-s">|  mount point  |  fstype  |  device  |  [device2]  |  [options - flags]</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">There are many Mediatek Chipset devices and for this reason, the following example can be used, but you should be aware that you MUST COMPARE the example with the stock recovery.fstab file and make all necessary changes in the twrp.flag file.</p>
 <details><summary>Example - Click to open</summary>
 <p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre>   <span class="pl-c"># Superimage Logical Partitions</span>
   <span class="pl-sr">/system_image   emmc      /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">system</span>       <span class="pl-s1">flags</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">display</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"System_Image"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">backup</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">flashimg</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-en">slotselect</span>
   <span class="pl-sr">/vendor_image   emmc      /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>       <span class="pl-s1">flags</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">display</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"Vendor_Image"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">backup</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">flashimg</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-en">slotselect</span>
   <span class="pl-sr">/product_image  emmc      /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">product</span>      <span class="pl-s1">flags</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">display</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"Product_Image"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">backup</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">flashimg</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-en">slotselect</span>

   <span class="pl-c"># Dynamic Partitions</span>
   <span class="pl-sr">/system_root   ext4   /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">system</span>      <span class="pl-s1">flags</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">display</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"System"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">backup</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">0</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-en">wipeingui</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-en">slotselect</span>
   <span class="pl-sr">/vendor        ext4   /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>      <span class="pl-s1">flags</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">display</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"Vendor"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">backup</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">0</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-en">wipeingui</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-en">slotselect</span>
   <span class="pl-sr">/product       ext4   /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">product</span>     <span class="pl-s1">flags</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">display</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"Product"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">backup</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">0</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-en">wipeingui</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-en">slotselect</span>


   <span class="pl-c"># mount point       fstype            device                                              flags</span>
   <span class="pl-sr">/protect_f          ext4      /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">platform</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">protect1</span>         <span class="pl-s1">flags</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">display</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"protect_f"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">backup</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span>
   <span class="pl-sr">/protect_s          ext4      /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">platform</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">protect2</span>         <span class="pl-s1">flags</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">display</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"protect_s"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">backup</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span>
   <span class="pl-sr">/nvdata             ext4      /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">platform</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">nvdata</span>           <span class="pl-s1">flags</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">display</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"NvData"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">backup</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span>
   <span class="pl-sr">/nvcfg              ext4      /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">platform</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">nvcfg</span>            <span class="pl-s1">flags</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">display</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"NvCfg"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">backup</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span>

   <span class="pl-sr">/persist_image      emmc      /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">platform</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>          <span class="pl-s1">flags</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">display</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"Persist_Image"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">backup</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">flashimg</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span>
   <span class="pl-sr">/persistent         emmc      /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">platform</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">frp</span>              <span class="pl-s1">flags</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">display</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"frp"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">backup</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">flashimg</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span>
   <span class="pl-sr">/nvram              emmc      /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">platform</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">nvram</span>            <span class="pl-s1">flags</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">display</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"NvRam"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">backup</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">flashimg</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span>

   <span class="pl-sr">/boot               emmc      /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">platform</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">boot</span>             <span class="pl-s1">flags</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">display</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"boot"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">flashimg</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-en">slotselect</span>
