   <span class="pl-c"># External Storage</span>
   <span class="pl-sr">/external_sd      auto      /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">mmcblk1p1</span>      /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">mmcblk1</span>      <span class="pl-s1">flags</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">display</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"MicroSDCard"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-en">storage</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-en">wipeingui</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-en">removable</span>
   <span class="pl-sr">/usb-otg          auto      /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">sda1</span>      /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">sda</span>      <span class="pl-s1">flags</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">display</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"Usb_Otg"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-en">storage</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-en">wipeingui</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-en">removable</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span><span class="pl-s1">fsflags</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"utf8"</span></pre></div>
 <p dir="auto"></p>
 </details>
<p dir="auto">If the Custom Recovery not mount MicroSDCard and or OTG, you can <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/3c1f3eaa66248c56e61acf19ddd4b96c#ii-get-some-informations-from-device-using-adb-fastboot-platform-tool">get some informations</a> from device using some tools like adb-fastboot, termux (need ROOT), ADB RUN or any other good tool. Insert MicroSDCard or and OTG of any size so that the external_sd / usb-otg address can look and later you should change correctlly in the twrp.flag file.
The example below show how have the correct sizes.</p>
 <details><summary>Example - Click to open</summary>
 <p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-yaml" dir="auto"><pre>   <span class="pl-s">c:\adb>adb -s XXXXXXXXX shell cat /proc/partitions`</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">XXXXXXXXX ➡️ It's the device serial number.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-yaml" dir="auto"><pre>   <span class="pl-s">c:\adb>adb devices</span>
   <span class="pl-s">XXXXXXXXX        authorized</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://gist.github.com/assets/25868008/b59b18c4-bda5-4cfd-8185-9f4db0e55d7b"><img src="https://gist.github.com/assets/25868008/b59b18c4-bda5-4cfd-8185-9f4db0e55d7b" alt="ksnip_20240211-011159" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
 <p dir="auto"></p>
 </details>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/vintf" rel="nofollow"><code>vintf\</code></a></p>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/vintf/objects" rel="nofollow"><code>manifest.xml</code></a></p>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Copy the file from the same stock system.img in the \system\etc\vintf folder. You can add more files like <code>compatibility_matrix.xml</code> if you want.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto"><code>lib64\</code></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto"><code>hw\</code></p>
<p dir="auto"><code>android.hardware.boot@1.0-impl-1.2-mtkimpl.so</code></p>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Adding something in the lib64\ folder depends on each OEM firmware and the processes need to work. Copy the file from the same stock recovery_ramdisk\system\lib64\hw folder.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-tip" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg class="octicon octicon-light-bulb mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M8 1.5c-2.363 0-4 1.69-4 3.75 0 .984.424 1.625.984 2.304l.214.253c.223.264.47.556.673.848.284.411.537.896.621 1.49a.75.75 0 0 1-1.484.211c-.04-.282-.163-.547-.37-.847a8.456 8.456 0 0 0-.542-.68c-.084-.1-.173-.205-.268-.32C3.201 7.75 2.5 6.766 2.5 5.25 2.5 2.31 4.863 0 8 0s5.5 2.31 5.5 5.25c0 1.516-.701 2.5-1.328 3.259-.095.115-.184.22-.268.319-.207.245-.383.453-.541.681-.208.3-.33.565-.37.847a.751.751 0 0 1-1.485-.212c.084-.593.337-1.078.621-1.489.203-.292.45-.584.673-.848.075-.088.147-.173.213-.253.561-.679.985-1.32.985-2.304 0-2.06-1.637-3.75-4-3.75ZM5.75 12h4.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-4.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0-1.5ZM6 15.25a.75.75 0 0 1 .75-.75h2.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-2.5a.75.75 0 0 1-.75-.75Z"></path></svg>Tip</p><p dir="auto">Pay attention to the version of the stock file <code>android.hardware.boot@*N.*n-impl-*N.*n-mtkimpl.so</code> as they may be different due to the Android version.</p>
</div>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 class="heading-element" dir="auto">2.3.5 \recovery\root\vendor\</h4><a id="user-content-235-recoveryrootvendor" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 2.3.5 \recovery\root\vendor\" href="#235-recoveryrootvendor"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto"><code>ueventd.rc</code></p>
<p dir="auto">Copy the file from the same stock vendor.img in the \vendor folder if firmware file have recovery.img file.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto"><code>etc\</code></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto"><code>vintf\</code></p>
<p dir="auto"><code>manifest.xml</code></p>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Copy the file from the same stock vendor.img in the \vendor\etc\vintf folder. You can add more files like <code>compatibility_matrix.xml</code> if you want.</p>
<p dir="auto"><code>ueventd.rc</code></p>
<p dir="auto">With firmware file have only boot.img file or have vendor_boot.img file, copy the stock <code>\vendor\etc\ueventd.rc</code> file from the stock vendor.img file to device tree <code>vendor\etc</code> folder.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto"><code>firmware\</code></p>
<p dir="auto">As we know from the InfoGuide <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/685cdd9c71476e9daf95ca612066e23f#touchscreen-not-working-in-custom-recovery-on-mediatek-devices---mtk---mtxxxx">Touchscreen not working</a> for Android 10+ version, the <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/685cdd9c71476e9daf95ca612066e23f#current-conditions">firmware folder</a> can have hardware binary files of the stock vendor.img file. These <code>*.bin</code> files will be useful for mainly enabling touch screen. But if the correct files are not in that folder, you should check the stock vendor.img file in the <code>\vendor\lib\modules\</code> folder.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto"><code>lib\</code></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>modules\</code></li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">As we know from the InfoGuide <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/685cdd9c71476e9daf95ca612066e23f#touchscreen-not-working-in-custom-recovery-on-mediatek-devices---mtk---mtxxxx">Touchscreen not working</a> for Android 10+ version, the <code>*.ko</code> files with hardware modules is located in <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/685cdd9c71476e9daf95ca612066e23f#current-conditions">certain folders</a> of the stock vendor.img file in the <code>\vendor\lib\modules\</code> folder. These <code>*.ko</code> files will be useful for mainly enabling touch screen.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">If the correct files are not in that folder, you should check the firmware file if have vendor_dlkm.img file and so confirm in the <code>\vendor_dlkm\lib\modules\</code> folder.</p>
<hr>
<p dir="auto">To better understand the organization of the structure with files, the example below is for an MT6833 device:</p>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://gist.github.com/assets/25868008/a67d71dc-e956-4741-8a16-78f42fb58425"><img src="https://gist.github.com/assets/25868008/a67d71dc-e956-4741-8a16-78f42fb58425" alt="ksnip_20240210-112914" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<hr>

So understand that if your Custom Recovery has problems that cannot be easily resolved, then it is time to ask for help and find experts or experienced developers.</p>
</div>

      <div id="file-8-crypt-decrypt-process-files-md-readme" class="Box-body readme blob tmp-p-5 tmp-p-xl-6 gist-border-0" style="overflow: auto" tabindex="0" role="region" aria-label="8. Crypt-Decrypt process&files.md content, created by lopestom on 02:09AM on January 24, 2024.">
    <article class="markdown-body entry-content container-lg" itemprop="text"><div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 class="heading-element" dir="auto">3. Crypt-Decrypt</h2><a id="user-content-3-crypt-decrypt" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 3. Crypt-Decrypt" href="#3-crypt-decrypt"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Many times, even with all the correct files and processes, there is a dependency on the Custom Recovery source code. Obviously, Custom Recovery being a type of OS, it needs to interact correctly and be updated in order to have functionality according to the Android version.</p>
<p dir="auto">So being sure which files and how the encryption process works is important. Having confidence and conviction that everything is right makes you understand the limits on the Custom Recovery source code. Don't get frustrated if something doesn't work. Sometimes the Custom Recovery source code takes time to update, but in this case you must confirm who failed.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 class="heading-element" dir="auto">3.1 Process & Files</h2><a id="user-content-31-process--files" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 3.1 Process & Files" href="#31-process--files"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The way the encryption process occurs and how it can be decrypted is not the subject here! This requires a lot of programming knowledge and experience with firmware.
Note that depending on the Android version originally shipped to the device, encryption modes may have <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/security/features/encryption/full-disk" rel="nofollow">FDE</a> (deprecated in Android 11+) and currently <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/security/features/encryption/file-based" rel="nofollow">FBE</a>. The files to enable these modes can have FDE binaries + gatekeeper or FBE binaries + gatekeeper.</p>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-important" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg class="octicon octicon-report mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M0 1.75C0 .784.784 0 1.75 0h12.5C15.216 0 16 .784 16 1.75v9.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.25 13H8.06l-2.573 2.573A1.458 1.458 0 0 1 3 14.543V13H1.75A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 0 11.25Zm1.75-.25a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v9.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h2a.75.75 0 0 1 .75.75v2.19l2.72-2.72a.749.749 0 0 1 .53-.22h6.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-9.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25-.25Zm7 2.25v2.5a.75.75 0 0 1-1.5 0v-2.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0ZM9 9a1 1 0 1 1-2 0 1 1 0 0 1 2 0Z"></path></svg>Important</p><p dir="auto">This InfoGuide is only intended to highlight <code>FBE mode</code> how <strong>BoardConfig.mk</strong> is set in the <a href="https://gist.github.com/lopestom/c4a2648958db5c3db03d32033a3583cd#file-3-1-boardconfig-mk_-md"><code># Crypto</code></a> item. It can serve as something similar for FDE mode, but the flags must be different.</p>
</div>
<p dir="auto">The fact is that there can be many encryption types, some of which are obsolete, others continue in a different form and others are still in use, and may have updates depending on the kernel and Android version. But currently there are three types of encryption for most Mediatek devices. In this document there will be no guidance for any type of encryption for a specific manufacturing company, because many devices from the same company may have different types of encryption.</p>
<p dir="auto">First we must know what type of encryption is applied to the device. There are a few ways to identify that.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Unpack the stock recovery ramdisk img file and open the <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file. Find the <code>trust</code> or <code>trustkernel</code> or <code>tee</code> or <code>beanpod</code> or <code>trustonic</code> line;</li>
<li>Unzip the stock super.img\vendor.img file and find the <code>\bin\hw</code> folder look if have is some file called <code>*-service.beanpod</code> or <code>*-service.trustkernel</code> or <code>*-service.trustonic</code>;</li>
<li>Unzip the stock super.img\vendor.img file and find the <code>\etc\init</code> folder look if have is some file called <code>microtrust.rc</code> or <code>trustkernel.rc</code> or <code>trustonic.rc</code>;</li>
<li>With ROOT you can use adb with the <code>adb getprop</code> command to obtain information from the <strong><em>prop.default</em></strong> file and thus search for the type of encryption.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-tip" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg class="octicon octicon-light-bulb mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M8 1.5c-2.363 0-4 1.69-4 3.75 0 .984.424 1.625.984 2.304l.214.253c.223.264.47.556.673.848.284.411.537.896.621 1.49a.75.75 0 0 1-1.484.211c-.04-.282-.163-.547-.37-.847a8.456 8.456 0 0 0-.542-.68c-.084-.1-.173-.205-.268-.32C3.201 7.75 2.5 6.766 2.5 5.25 2.5 2.31 4.863 0 8 0s5.5 2.31 5.5 5.25c0 1.516-.701 2.5-1.328 3.259-.095.115-.184.22-.268.319-.207.245-.383.453-.541.681-.208.3-.33.565-.37.847a.751.751 0 0 1-1.485-.212c.084-.593.337-1.078.621-1.489.203-.292.45-.584.673-.848.075-.088.147-.173.213-.253.561-.679.985-1.32.985-2.304 0-2.06-1.637-3.75-4-3.75ZM5.75 12h4.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-4.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0-1.5ZM6 15.25a.75.75 0 0 1 .75-.75h2.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-2.5a.75.75 0 0 1-.75-.75Z"></path></svg>Tip</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto"><strong>How to know which binary files are dependent on other binary files?</strong></p>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://github.com/TeamWin/android_device_qcom_twrp-common#using-ldcheck-to-find-dependencies">Read and learn</a> how to use ldcheck. Change the file reference in the <code>vendor\bin\qseecomd</code> folder to its reference in the <code>vendor\bin\</code> folder of the file's encryption type.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">If you want to read and understand about Actions and Services as a way of executing processes, then read <a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform/system/core/+/master/init/README.md#android-init-language" rel="nofollow">Android Init Language</a>.</p>
</li>
</ul>
</div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 class="heading-element" dir="auto">3.2 Trustkernel</h2><a id="user-content-32-trustkernel" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 3.2 Trustkernel" href="#32-trustkernel"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">This type of encryption is not widely used. But when it exists on a device, it has been the most annoying and frustrating way to try to get it to work. Generally speaking, some developers with Android 9 devices were able to do something. But with the device shipping with Android 10, it didn't work the same way.
But a smart and hardy guy developer named @ADeadTrousers took a while for other users to have something actually working on Android 10 and Android 11.</p>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><p dir="auto">Understanding this, you should find some files related to <em>trustkernel</em>. These files come from the stock vendor.img file and must be located in:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>\vendor\bin</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>teed</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\vendor\bin\hw</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>android.hardware.gatekeeper@1.0-service</code></li>
<li><code>android.hardware.keymaster@4.0-service.trustkernel</code></li>
<li><code>vendor.mediatek.hardware.keymaster_attestation@1.1-service</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\vendor\etc\init</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>android.hardware.gatekeeper@1.0-service.rc</code></li>
<li><code>android.hardware.keymaster@4.0-service.trustkernel.rc</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\vendor\etc\vintf</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>manifest.xml</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\vendor\lib64</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>libkmsetkey.so</code></li>
<li><code>libteec.so</code></li>
<li><code>vendor.mediatek.hardware.keymaster_attestation@1.0.so</code></li>
<li><code>vendor.mediatek.hardware.keymaster_attestation@1.1.so</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\vendor\lib64\hw</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>android.hardware.gatekeeper@1.0-impl.so</code></li>
<li><code>gatekeeper.trustkernel.so</code></li>
<li><code>kmsetkey.trustkernel.so</code></li>
<li><code>libSoftGatekeeper.so</code></li>
<li><code>vendor.mediatek.hardware.keymaster_attestation@1.1-impl.so</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\vendor\app\t6</code> & <code>\vendor\app\t6\data</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Get the entire <code>\app</code> folder and all the files.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Pay attention to file updates! Example: The above <code>android.hardware.keymaster@4.0-service.trustkernel</code> file can change to <code>android.hardware.keymaster@4.1-service.trustkernel</code> or <code>android.hardware.security.keymint-service.trustkernel.rc</code>. So you must modify every part of files where this <code>*-service.trustkernel</code> file is being referenced.</p>
<p dir="auto">There are other files, but they are optional (maybe):</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>\vendor\bin</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>kph</code></li>
<li><code>pld</code></li>
<li><code>tee_check_keybox</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\vendor\lib64</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>libkphhelper.so</code></li>
<li><code>libkphproxy.so</code></li>
<li><code>libpl.so</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">As a preference, get a <a href="https://github.com/ADeadTrousers/twrp_device_Unihertz_Atom_LXL/tree/twrp-11.0">device tree</a> that actually works, even if it is to compile the recovery.img file. It has been tested by compiling the Custom Recovery in boot.img file and it works. So don't forget to thank and give credits to @ADeadTrousers.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>android\brand\devicename\recovery\root<br>
There may be differences between devices, so open the stock <strong>ramdisk\init.recovery.{hardware}.rc</strong> file and note the <code>on post-fs</code> line in the init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc file below.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</p>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre> <span class="pl-en">import</span> <span class="pl-sr">/init.recovery.trustkernel.rc</span>
<span class="pl-sr"> import /vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">etc</span>/<span class="pl-en">init</span>/<span class="pl-en">trustkernel</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">rc</span>
 <span class="pl-en">import</span> <span class="pl-sr">/vendor/etc</span>/<span class="pl-en">init</span>/<span class="pl-en">android</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">hardware</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">gatekeeper</span>@<span class="pl-c1">1.0</span>-<span class="pl-en">service</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">rc</span>
 <span class="pl-en">import</span> <span class="pl-sr">/vendor/etc</span>/<span class="pl-en">init</span>/<span class="pl-en">android</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">hardware</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">keymaster</span>@<span class="pl-c1">4.0</span>-<span class="pl-en">service</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">trustkernel</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">rc</span>
    
 <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">post</span>-<span class="pl-en">fs</span>
 <span class="pl-c"># **COPY YOUR STOCK ramdisk\init.recovery.{hardware}.rc `on post-fs` FILE HERE!!**</span>
 
  <span class="pl-en">start</span> <span class="pl-en">mtk</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">plpath</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">utils</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">link</span>

 <span class="pl-en">service</span> <span class="pl-en">mtk</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">plpath</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">utils</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">link</span> <span class="pl-sr">/system/bin</span>/<span class="pl-en">mtk_plpath_utils</span>
     <span class="pl-k">class</span> <span class="pl-en">main</span>
     <span class="pl-en">user</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span>
     <span class="pl-en">group</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span>
     <span class="pl-en">disabled</span>
     <span class="pl-en">oneshot</span>
     <span class="pl-en">seclabel</span> <span class="pl-pds">u</span><span class="pl-pds">:r</span><span class="pl-pds">:recovery</span><span class="pl-pds">:s0</span>

 <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">boot</span>
     <span class="pl-en">start</span> <span class="pl-en">boot</span>-<span class="pl-en">hal</span>-<span class="pl-c1">1</span>-<span class="pl-c1">1</span>
     <span class="pl-en">start</span> <span class="pl-en">health</span>-<span class="pl-en">hal</span>-<span class="pl-c1">2</span>-<span class="pl-c1">1</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">init.recovery.trustkernel.rc</p>
<p dir="auto">This is the script file that performs the decryption and is referenced (import) line in the <code>init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</code> file. Note that there are processes and phases of file targeting so that decryption can be done.</p>

<p dir="auto">Some text files are important to know how they are written and to place inside the <code>init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</code> file like <code>android.hardware.gatekeeper@1.0-service.rc</code> and <code>android.hardware.keymaster@4.0-service.trustkernel.rc</code>. You can choose to remove these files from the folder and place them inside the <code>init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</code> file. This is optional. So if you do that, remove the reference (symlink) line from the same file in the <code>init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</code> file. Remove the aforementioned files from the <code>\vendor\etc\init</code> folder so there is no confusion.</p>
<p dir="auto">Add the <a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform/system/core/+/master/init/README.md#options" rel="nofollow">line below</a> so that Custom Recovery has permission over the process. This should happen for all <code>*-service</code> files.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre>     <span class="pl-en">seclabel</span> <span class="pl-pds">u</span>:<span class="pl-en">r</span>:<span class="pl-pds">recovery</span>:<span class="pl-en">s0</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto">As we will copy what the script author did, it is necessary to compare the stock file <code>vendor\etc\init\trustkernel.rc</code> with the file below. If there are any modifications to be made, then do as the stock file has.</p>
  <details><summary>Click to open</summary>
  <p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre>  <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">post</span>-<span class="pl-en">fs</span>
      <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/proc/bootprof</span> <span class="pl-s">"tkcore: prepare system ta path"</span>

      <span class="pl-en">restorecon</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>
      <span class="pl-en">mkdir</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6_twrp</span>
      <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6_twrp</span>
      <span class="pl-en">restorecon</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6_twrp</span>

      <span class="pl-en">restorecon</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">protect_f</span>
      <span class="pl-en">mkdir</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">protect_f</span>/<span class="pl-en">tee_twrp</span>
      <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">protect_f</span>/<span class="pl-en">tee_twrp</span>
      <span class="pl-en">restorecon</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">protect_f</span>/<span class="pl-en">tee_twrp</span>

      <span class="pl-en">start</span> <span class="pl-en">teed</span>

  <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-pds">property</span>:<span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">trustkernel</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">fs</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">state</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-en">prepare</span>
      <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/proc/bootprof</span> <span class="pl-s">"tkcore: prepare basic"</span>
      <span class="pl-en">mkdir</span> <span class="pl-sr">/data/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6</span>
      <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/data/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6</span>
      <span class="pl-en">restorecon</span> <span class="pl-sr">/data/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6</span>

      <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/proc/bootprof</span> <span class="pl-s">"tkcore: prepare sfs"</span>
      <span class="pl-en">mkdir</span> <span class="pl-sr">/data/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6</span>/<span class="pl-en">fs</span>
      <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/data/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6</span>/<span class="pl-en">fs</span>
      <span class="pl-en">restorecon</span> <span class="pl-sr">/data/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6</span>/<span class="pl-en">fs</span>

      <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/proc/bootprof</span> <span class="pl-s">"tkcore: prepare service provider ta path"</span>
      <span class="pl-en">mkdir</span> <span class="pl-sr">/data/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6</span>/<span class="pl-en">app</span>
      <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/data/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6</span>/<span class="pl-en">app</span>
      <span class="pl-en">restorecon</span> <span class="pl-sr">/data/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6</span>/<span class="pl-en">app</span>
      <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">trustkernel</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">fs</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">state</span> <span class="pl-en">ready</span>

  <span class="pl-c"># For non-encrypted case</span>
  <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-pds">property</span>:<span class="pl-en">ro</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">crypto</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">state</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-en">unencrypted</span>
      <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">trustkernel</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">fs</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">mode</span> <span class="pl-c1">1</span>
      <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">trustkernel</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">fs</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">state</span> <span class="pl-en">prepare</span>

  <span class="pl-c"># For FDE/encrypted successfully</span>
  <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-pds">property</span>:<span class="pl-en">vold</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">decrypt</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-en">trigger_restart_framework</span>
      <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">trustkernel</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">fs</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">mode</span> <span class="pl-c1">2</span>
      <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">trustkernel</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">fs</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">state</span> <span class="pl-en">prepare</span>

  <span class="pl-c"># For FBE/encrypted successfully</span>
  <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-pds">property</span>:<span class="pl-en">ro</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">crypto</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">type</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-en">file</span> && <span class="pl-en">property</span>:<span class="pl-en">ro</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">crypto</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">state</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-en">encrypted</span>
      <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">trustkernel</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">fs</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">mode</span> <span class="pl-c1">3</span>
      <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">trustkernel</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">fs</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">state</span> <span class="pl-en">prepare</span>

  <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-pds">property</span>:<span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">trustkernel</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">log</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">state</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-en">ready</span>
      <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/proc/bootprof</span> <span class="pl-s">"tkcore: prepare log file"</span>
      <span class="pl-en">restorecon</span> <span class="pl-sr">/data/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6</span>/<span class="pl-en">tkcore</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">log</span>
      <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">trustkernel</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">log</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">state</span> <span class="pl-en">enable</span>

  <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-pds">property</span>:<span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">trustkernel</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">ready</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">true</span>
      <span class="pl-en">start</span> <span class="pl-en">tee_check_keybox</span>

  <span class="pl-en">service</span> <span class="pl-en">teed</span> <span class="pl-sr">/vendor/bin</span>/<span class="pl-en">teed</span> \
      --<span class="pl-en">datapath</span> /<span class="pl-en">data</span>/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6</span>/<span class="pl-en">fs</span> \
      --<span class="pl-en">sptapath</span> /<span class="pl-en">data</span>/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6</span>/<span class="pl-en">app</span> \
      --<span class="pl-en">systapath</span> /<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">app</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6</span> \
      --<span class="pl-en">rpmbdev</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">mmcblk0rpmb</span> \
      --<span class="pl-en">rpmbdev</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">rpmb0</span> \
      --<span class="pl-en">prot</span> /<span class="pl-en">mnt</span>/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6_twrp</span> \
      --<span class="pl-en">prot</span> /<span class="pl-en">mnt</span>/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">protect_f</span>/<span class="pl-en">tee_twrp</span> \
      --<span class="pl-en">logpath</span> /<span class="pl-en">data</span>/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6</span>/<span class="pl-en">tkcore</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">log</span> \
      --<span class="pl-en">proprefix</span> <span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">trustkernel</span>
      <span class="pl-en">capabilities</span> <span class="pl-c1">SYS_RAWIO</span>
      <span class="pl-en">user</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span>
      <span class="pl-en">group</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span>
      <span class="pl-k">class</span> <span class="pl-en">core</span>
      <span class="pl-en">seclabel</span> <span class="pl-pds">u</span><span class="pl-pds">:r</span><span class="pl-pds">:recovery</span><span class="pl-pds">:s0</span>

  <span class="pl-en">service</span> <span class="pl-en">tee_check_keybox</span> <span class="pl-sr">/vendor/bin</span>/<span class="pl-en">tee_check_keybox</span>
      <span class="pl-en">user</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span>
      <span class="pl-en">group</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span>
      <span class="pl-k">class</span> <span class="pl-en">main</span>
      <span class="pl-en">disabled</span>
      <span class="pl-en">oneshot</span>
      <span class="pl-en">seclabel</span> <span class="pl-pds">u</span><span class="pl-pds">:r</span><span class="pl-pds">:recovery</span><span class="pl-pds">:s0</span></pre></div>
  <p dir="auto"></p>
  </details>
<p dir="auto">If everything is ok, then copy the above script with the name <code>trustkernel.rc</code> as it came from stock in the same folder in the device tree.</p>
<p dir="auto">Again there is <a href="https://github.com/ADeadTrousers/twrp_device_Unihertz_Atom_LXL/blob/twrp-11.0/recovery/root/vendor/bin/trustkernel.twrp.sh">another script file</a> that the author made, but we just copied it to the <code>android\brand\devicename\recovery\root\vendor\bin\trustkernel.twrp.sh</code> folder as you can see below.</p>
  <details><summary>Click to open</summary>
  <p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre>   <span class="pl-c">#!/sbin/sh</span>

    <span class="pl-en">rm</span> -<span class="pl-en">rf</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6_twrp</span>/*
    <span class="pl-s1">cp</span> -<span class="pl-en">rfp</span> /<span class="pl-en">mnt</span>/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6</span>/* <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">t6_twrp</span>

    <span class="pl-en">rm</span> -<span class="pl-en">rf</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">protect_f</span>/<span class="pl-en">tee_twrp</span>/*
    <span class="pl-s1">cp</span> -<span class="pl-en">rfp</span> /<span class="pl-en">mnt</span>/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">protect_f</span>/<span class="pl-en">tee</span>/* <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">protect_f</span>/<span class="pl-en">tee_twrp</span></pre></div>
  <p dir="auto"></p>
  </details>
<p dir="auto">Files come from the stock system.img file and must be located in:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>\system\etc\vintf</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>manifest.xml</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Copy all folders and all files within <code>\vendor</code> to the device tree under <code>android\brand\devicename\recovery\root\vendor\</code> and all files within <code>\system</code> to the device tree under <code>android\brand\devicename\recovery\root\system\</code>. Remember that the files <code>init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</code> and <code>init.recovery.trustkernel.rc</code> must be in <code>android\brand\devicename\recovery\root</code>.</p>
<p dir="auto">To better understand the organization of the structure with encryption/decryption files, an example below for an MT6762 device:</p>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/25868008/302822788-a548171d-2106-450a-83ed-a5326f47a558.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..dvdv69oXAuXRmguR2d9mIvwDlb-QErH8L163rw091BY"><img src="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/25868008/302822788-a548171d-2106-450a-83ed-a5326f47a558.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..dvdv69oXAuXRmguR2d9mIvwDlb-QErH8L163rw091BY" alt="ksnip_20240206-215700_resized" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<p dir="auto">Currently I have seen two devices shipping with Android 12 - kernel 5.1.xxx which has <em>trustkernel</em>. But it hasn't worked yet (tests in one device MT6789 carried out until October 2023) as far as I know. Maybe someone sucessded.</p>
</article>
  </div>

  </div>

</div>

      <div id="file-8-1-crypt-decrypt-process-files-md-readme" class="Box-body readme blob tmp-p-5 tmp-p-xl-6 gist-border-0" style="overflow: auto" tabindex="0" role="region" aria-label="8.1 Crypt-Decrypt process&files.md content, created by lopestom on 02:09AM on January 24, 2024.">
    <article class="markdown-body entry-content container-lg" itemprop="text"><div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 class="heading-element" dir="auto">3.3 Beanpod (microtrust)</h2><a id="user-content-33-beanpod-microtrust" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 3.3 Beanpod (microtrust)" href="#33-beanpod-microtrust"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">This type of encryption is one of the smoothest types there is to run on a device. Not only because of the simplicity of just a few files, but because since before Android 9, it has always worked without difficulties and extra efforts. Well, for Android 11+ versions it all depends on the Custom Recovery source code.</p>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><p dir="auto">Understanding this, you should find some files related to <em>beanpod</em>. These files come from the stock vendor.img file and must be located in:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>\vendor\bin</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>teei_daemon</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\vendor\bin\hw</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>android.hardware.gatekeeper@1.0-service</code></li>
<li><code>android.hardware.keymaster@4.1-service.beanpod</code></li>
<li><code>vendor.mediatek.hardware.keymaster_attestation@1.1-service</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\vendor\etc\vintf</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>manifest.xml</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\vendor\lib64</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>libimsg_log.so</code></li>
<li><code>libTEECommon.so</code></li>
<li><code>libteei_daemon_vfs.so</code></li>
<li><code>vendor.mediatek.hardware.keymaster_attestation@1.0.so</code></li>
<li><code>vendor.mediatek.hardware.keymaster_attestation@1.1.so</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\vendor\lib64\hw</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>android.hardware.gatekeeper@1.0-impl.so</code></li>
<li><code>gatekeeper.beanpod.so</code></li>
<li><code>gatekeeper.default.so</code></li>
<li><code>kmsetkey.beanpod.so</code></li>
<li><code>libSoftGatekeeper.so</code></li>
<li><code>vendor.mediatek.hardware.keymaster_attestation@1.1-impl.so</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\vendor\thh\ta</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Get the entire <code>\thh</code> folder and all the files.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Pay attention to file updates! Example: The above <code>android.hardware.keymaster@4.1-service.beanpod</code> file can change to <code>android.hardware.keymaster@4.0-service.beanpod</code> or <code>android.hardware.security.keymint-service.beanpod.rc</code>. So you must modify every part of files where this <code>*-service.beanpod</code> file is being referenced.</p>
<p dir="auto">In tests carried out with Custom Recoveries, they did not need to include files with <code>vendor.mediatek.hardware.keymaster_attestation**</code>, as decryption worked without them. So that's optional.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>android\brand\devicename\recovery\root<br>
There may be differences between devices, so open the stock <strong>ramdisk\init.recovery.{hardware}.rc</strong> file and note the line <code>on post-fs</code> through <code>on post-fs</code> in the init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc file below.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</p>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre>   <span class="pl-en">import</span> <span class="pl-sr">/init.recovery.microtrust.rc</span>
<span class="pl-sr"></span>
<span class="pl-sr">   on init</span>
<span class="pl-sr">       # Create a more standard /dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span> <span class="pl-en">layout</span> <span class="pl-k">for</span> <span class="pl-en">our</span> <span class="pl-en">scripts</span>
       <span class="pl-en">symlink</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/block</span>/<span class="pl-en">platform</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/block</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>
       <span class="pl-en">export</span> <span class="pl-c1">LD_LIBRARY_PATH</span> <span class="pl-sr">/system/lib</span><span class="pl-c1">64</span><span class="pl-pds">:/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-pds">lib64</span><span class="pl-pds">:/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">lib64</span>/<span class="pl-pds">hw</span><span class="pl-pds">:/system</span>/<span class="pl-en">lib64</span>/<span class="pl-en">hw</span>

   <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">fs</span>
       <span class="pl-en">mkdir</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span> <span class="pl-c1">0771</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span>
       <span class="pl-en">mount</span> <span class="pl-en">ext4</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/block</span>/<span class="pl-en">platform</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span> /<span class="pl-en">mnt</span>/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>

       <span class="pl-en">install_keyring</span>
       <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">crypto</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">ready</span> <span class="pl-c1">1</span>

   <span class="pl-en">service</span> <span class="pl-en">keystore_auth</span> <span class="pl-sr">/system/bin</span>/<span class="pl-en">keystore_auth</span>
       <span class="pl-en">oneshot</span>
       <span class="pl-en">user</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span>
       <span class="pl-en">group</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span>
       <span class="pl-en">disabled</span>
       <span class="pl-en">seclabel</span> <span class="pl-pds">u</span>:<span class="pl-en">r</span>:<span class="pl-pds">recovery</span>:<span class="pl-en">s0</span>

   <span class="pl-en">service</span> <span class="pl-en">keystore</span> <span class="pl-sr">/system/bin</span>/<span class="pl-en">keystore</span> /<span class="pl-en">tmp</span>/<span class="pl-en">misc</span>/<span class="pl-en">keystore</span>
       <span class="pl-en">user</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span>
       <span class="pl-en">group</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span> <span class="pl-en">drmrpc</span> <span class="pl-en">readproc</span>
       <span class="pl-en">disabled</span>
       <span class="pl-en">seclabel</span> <span class="pl-pds">u</span>:<span class="pl-en">r</span>:<span class="pl-pds">recovery</span>:<span class="pl-en">s0</span>

   <span class="pl-en">service</span> <span class="pl-en">gatekeeper</span>-<span class="pl-c1">1</span>-<span class="pl-c1">0</span> /<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">bin</span>/<span class="pl-en">hw</span>/<span class="pl-en">android</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">hardware</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">gatekeeper</span>@<span class="pl-c1">1.0</span>-<span class="pl-en">service</span>
       <span class="pl-en">interface</span> <span class="pl-en">android</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">hardware</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">gatekeeper</span>@<span class="pl-c1">1.0</span>::<span class="pl-en">IGatekeeper</span> <span class="pl-en">default</span>
       <span class="pl-k">class</span> <span class="pl-en">hal</span>

<p dir="auto">Some text files are important to know how they are written and place them inside the <code>init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</code> file, such as <code>android.hardware.gatekeeper@1.0-service.rc</code> and <code>android.hardware.keymaster@4.1-service.beanpod.rc</code>. You can choose to remove these files from the folder and place them inside the <code>init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</code> file. This is optional and <strong>please note</strong> this is what I did. Also note that there is no inclusion (import) line of the same file in <code>init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</code> related to the files mentioned in the stock folder <code>\vendor\etc\init</code>.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">init.recovery.microtrust.rc</p>
<p dir="auto">This is the script file that performs the decryption and is referenced (import) line in the <code>init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</code> file. Note that there are processes and phases of file targeting so that decryption can be done.
It is necessary to compare the stock file <code>vendor\etc\init\microtrust.rc</code> with the file below. If there are any modifications to be made, do so according to the stock file.</p>

     <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">rpmb</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chmod</span> <span class="pl-c1">0771</span> /<span class="pl-en">mnt</span>/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">rpmb</span>
     <span class="pl-en">restorecon_recursive</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">rpmb</span>

     <span class="pl-c"># Set other rpmb</span>
     <span class="pl-en">mkdir</span> <span class="pl-sr">/persist/rpmb</span>
     <span class="pl-en">mkdir</span> <span class="pl-sr">/vendor/persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">rpmb</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chmod</span> <span class="pl-c1">0771</span> /<span class="pl-en">persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">rpmb</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chmod</span> <span class="pl-c1">0771</span> /<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">rpmb</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/persist/rpmb</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/vendor/persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">rpmb</span>
     <span class="pl-en">restorecon_recursive</span> <span class="pl-sr">/persist</span>
<span class="pl-sr">     restorecon_recursive /vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>

 <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">fs</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chmod</span> <span class="pl-c1">0660</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">teei_client</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/teei</span><span class="pl-en">_client</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chmod</span> <span class="pl-c1">0660</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">teei_config</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/teei</span><span class="pl-en">_config</span>
     <span class="pl-c"># 666 for SVP</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chmod</span> <span class="pl-c1">0666</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">isee_tee0</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">drmrpc</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/isee</span><span class="pl-en">_tee0</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chmod</span> <span class="pl-c1">0660</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">tz_vfs</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/tz</span><span class="pl-en">_vfs</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chmod</span> <span class="pl-c1">0660</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">teei_fp</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/teei</span><span class="pl-en">_fp</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">drmrpc</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/ut</span><span class="pl-en">_keymaster</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chmod</span> <span class="pl-c1">0660</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">ut_keymaster</span>
     <span class="pl-c"># rpmb device</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chmod</span> <span class="pl-c1">0660</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">rpmb0</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/rpmb</span><span class="pl-c1">0</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chmod</span> <span class="pl-c1">0660</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">mmcblk0rpmb</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/mmcblk</span><span class="pl-c1">0</span><span class="pl-en">rpmb</span>
     <span class="pl-c"># legacy rpmb device for cross-platform compatibility</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chmod</span> <span class="pl-c1">0660</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">emmcrpmb0</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/emmcrpmb</span><span class="pl-c1">0</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/utr</span><span class="pl-en">_tui</span>
     <span class="pl-en">chmod</span> <span class="pl-c1">0660</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">utr_tui</span>
     <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/proc/bootprof</span> <span class="pl-s">"start teei_daemon (on fs)"</span>
     <span class="pl-en">start</span> <span class="pl-en">teei_daemon</span>
     <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/proc/bootprof</span> <span class="pl-s">"start teei_daemon end (on fs)"</span>

 <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-pds">property</span>:<span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">crypto</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">fake_encrypt</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span> && <span class="pl-en">property</span>:<span class="pl-en">vold</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">post_fs_data_done</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">1</span>
     <span class="pl-en">setprop</span> <span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">soter</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">teei</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">crypto</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">state</span> <span class="pl-en">unencrypted</span></pre></div>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Files come from the stock system.img file and must be located in:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>\system\etc\vintf</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>manifest.xml</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Copy all folders and all files within <code>\vendor</code> to the device tree under <code>android\brand\devicename\recovery\root\vendor\</code> and all files within <code>\system</code> to the device tree under <code>android\brand\devicename\recovery\root\system\</code>. Remember that the files <code>init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</code> and <code>init.recovery.microtrust.rc</code> must be in <code>android\brand\devicename\recovery\root</code>.</p>
<p dir="auto">To better understand the organization of the structure with encryption/decryption files, an example below for an MT6877 device:</p>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/25868008/303156894-18181078-ff6d-495d-b21a-dd31872c22a2.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..2psReKVDqIrb9LDzkccHhTogIHhJ_ZMWLiG-Z9e2Bd4"><img src="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/25868008/303156894-18181078-ff6d-495d-b21a-dd31872c22a2.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..2psReKVDqIrb9LDzkccHhTogIHhJ_ZMWLiG-Z9e2Bd4" alt="ksnip_20240207-181841_resized" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<p dir="auto">From my experience, there are <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/kernel/android-common#feature-and-launch-kernels" rel="nofollow">devices shipped</a> with Android 11 with kernel 4.19, devices shipped with Android 12 with kernel 4.14 and with kernel 5.1.xxx that have beanpod with fully functional decryption.</p>
</article>
  </div>

  </div>

</div>

      <div id="file-8-2-crypt-decrypt-process-files-md-readme" class="Box-body readme blob tmp-p-5 tmp-p-xl-6 gist-border-0" style="overflow: auto" tabindex="0" role="region" aria-label="8.2 Crypt-Decrypt process&files.md content, created by lopestom on 02:09AM on January 24, 2024.">
    <article class="markdown-body entry-content container-lg" itemprop="text"><div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 class="heading-element" dir="auto">3.4 Trustonic</h2><a id="user-content-34-trustonic" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 3.4 Trustonic" href="#34-trustonic"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">This type of encryption is one of the most used in devices from the Infinix, Tecno, Realme and some Xiaomi-Poco brands. It has been used since before Android 9 and for the most part it has always worked well. So for Android 11+ versions, it all depends on the Custom Recovery source code.</p>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><p dir="auto">Understanding this, you should find some files related to <em>trustonic</em>. These files come from the stock vendor.img file and must be located in:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>\vendor\app\mcRegistry</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Get the entire <code>\app</code> folder and all the files.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\vendor\bin</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>mcDriverDaemon</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\vendor\bin\hw</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>android.hardware.gatekeeper@1.0-service</code></li>
<li><code>android.hardware.keymaster@4.0-service.trustonic</code></li>
<li><code>vendor.trustonic.tee@1.1-service</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\vendor\etc\vintf</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>manifest.xml</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\vendor\lib64</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>libkmsetkey.so</code></li>
<li><code>libladder.so</code></li>
<li><code>libMcClient.so</code></li>
<li><code>libTEECommon.so</code></li>
<li><code>vendor.trustonic.tee.tui@1.0.so</code></li>
<li><code>vendor.trustonic.tee@1.0.so</code></li>
<li><code>vendor.trustonic.tee@1.1.so</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\vendor\lib64\hw</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>android.hardware.gatekeeper@1.0-impl.so</code></li>
<li><code>gatekeeper.default.so</code></li>
<li><code>gatekeeper.trustonic.so</code></li>
<li><code>kmsetkey.default.so</code></li>
<li><code>kmsetkey.trustonic.so</code></li>
<li><code>libMcGatekeeper.so</code></li>
<li><code>libSoftGatekeeper.so</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Depending on the Android and kernel version, your device has a few more dependent binary files. You can check the <code>\vendor\</code> stock folders for the files in the:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>bin\</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>paytrigger_test</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>bin\hw\</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>vendor.mediatek.hardware.paytrigger@1.0-service</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>lib64\</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>libmtk_bsg.so</code></li>
<li><code>liburee_meta_drmkeyinstall.so</code></li>
<li><code>vendor.mediatek.hardware.paytrigger@1.0.so</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>lib\hw\</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>paytrigger.trustonic.so</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Pay attention to file updates! Example: The above <code>android.hardware.keymaster@4.0-service.trustonic</code> file can change to <code>android.hardware.keymaster@4.1-service.trustonic</code> or <code>android.hardware.security.keymint-service.trustonic.rc</code>. So you must modify every part of files where this <code>*-service.trustonic</code> file is being referenced.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>android\brand\devicename\recovery\root<br>
There may be differences between devices, so open the stock <strong>ramdisk\init.recovery.{hardware}.rc</strong> file and note the line <code>on post-fs</code> through <code>on post-fs</code> in the init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc file below.
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</p>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre>   <span class="pl-en">import</span> <span class="pl-sr">/trustonic.rc</span>
<span class="pl-sr">   import /init</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">tee</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">rc</span>
   
   <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">init</span>
       <span class="pl-en">symlink</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/block</span>/<span class="pl-en">platform</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span> /<span class="pl-en">dev</span>/<span class="pl-en">block</span>/<span class="pl-en">bootdevice</span>
       <span class="pl-en">export</span> <span class="pl-c1">LD_LIBRARY_PATH</span> <span class="pl-sr">/system/lib</span>:/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">lib</span>:/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">lib</span>/<span class="pl-en">hw</span>:/<span class="pl-en">system</span>/<span class="pl-en">lib</span>/<span class="pl-en">hw</span>

   <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">fs</span>
       <span class="pl-en">mkdir</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span> <span class="pl-c1">0700</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span>
       <span class="pl-en">mkdir</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span> <span class="pl-c1">0700</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span>
       <span class="pl-en">mount</span> <span class="pl-en">ext4</span> <span class="pl-sr">/dev/block</span>/<span class="pl-en">by</span>-<span class="pl-en">name</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span> /<span class="pl-en">mnt</span>/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>
 
   <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">post</span>-<span class="pl-en">fs</span>
           <span class="pl-c"># **COPY YOUR STOCK ramdisk\init.recovery.{hardware}.rc `on post-fs` FILE HERE!!**</span>


       <span class="pl-en">start</span> <span class="pl-en">mtk</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">plpath</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">utils</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">link</span>

   <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">fs</span>
       <span class="pl-en">install_keyring</span>

   <span class="pl-en">service</span> <span class="pl-en">mtk</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">plpath</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">utils</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">link</span> <span class="pl-sr">/system/bin</span>/<span class="pl-en">mtk_plpath_utils</span>
       <span class="pl-k">class</span> <span class="pl-en">main</span>
       <span class="pl-en">user</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span>
       <span class="pl-en">group</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span>
       <span class="pl-en">disabled</span>
       <span class="pl-en">oneshot</span>
       <span class="pl-en">seclabel</span> <span class="pl-pds">u</span><span class="pl-pds">:r</span><span class="pl-pds">:recovery</span><span class="pl-pds">:s0</span>

   <span class="pl-en">service</span> <span class="pl-en">keystore_auth</span> <span class="pl-sr">/system/bin</span>/<span class="pl-en">keystore_auth</span>
       <span class="pl-en">oneshot</span>
       <span class="pl-en">user</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span>
       <span class="pl-en">group</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span>
       <span class="pl-en">disabled</span>
       <span class="pl-en">seclabel</span> <span class="pl-pds">u</span><span class="pl-pds">:r</span><span class="pl-pds">:recovery</span><span class="pl-pds">:s0</span>

   <span class="pl-en">service</span> <span class="pl-en">keystore</span> <span class="pl-sr">/system/bin</span>/<span class="pl-en">keystore</span> <span class="pl-sr">/tmp/misc</span>/<span class="pl-en">keystore</span>
       <span class="pl-en">user</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span>
       <span class="pl-en">group</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span> <span class="pl-en">drmrpc</span> <span class="pl-en">readproc</span> <span class="pl-en">log</span>
       <span class="pl-en">disabled</span>
       <span class="pl-en">seclabel</span> <span class="pl-pds">u</span><span class="pl-pds">:r</span><span class="pl-pds">:recovery</span><span class="pl-pds">:s0</span>

   <span class="pl-en">service</span> <span class="pl-en">gatekeeper</span>-<span class="pl-c1">1</span>-<span class="pl-c1">0</span> <span class="pl-sr">/system/bin</span>/<span class="pl-en">android</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">hardware</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">gatekeeper</span>@<span class="pl-c1">1.0</span>-<span class="pl-en">service</span>
       <span class="pl-en">user</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span>
       <span class="pl-en">group</span> <span class="pl-en">root</span>
       <span class="pl-en">disabled</span>
       <span class="pl-en">seclabel</span> <span class="pl-en">u</span><span class="pl-pds">:r</span><span class="pl-pds">:recovery</span><span class="pl-pds">:s0</span>

   <span class="pl-en">service</span> <span class="pl-en">tee</span>-<span class="pl-c1">1</span>-<span class="pl-c1">1</span> <span class="pl-sr">/system/bin</span>/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">trustonic</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">tee</span>@<span class="pl-c1">1.1</span>-<span class="pl-en">service</span>

   <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">property</span><span class="pl-pds">:vendor</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">sys</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">listener</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">registered</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-c1">true</span>

<p dir="auto">Some text files are important to know how they are written and place them inside the <code>init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</code> file, such as <code>android.hardware.gatekeeper@1.0-service.rc</code> and <code>android.hardware.keymaster@4.0-service.trustonic.rc</code>. You can choose to remove these files from the folder and place them inside the <code>init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</code> file. This is optional and <strong>please note</strong> this is what I did. Also note that there is no inclusion (import) line of the same file in <code>init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</code> related to the files mentioned in the stock folder <code>\vendor\etc\init</code>.</p>
<p dir="auto">The files below are script files that perform decryption and are referenced in the (import) lines in the <code>init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</code> file.  Note that there are processes and phases of file targeting so that decryption can be done.
It is necessary to compare the stock files <code>vendor\etc\init\tee.rc</code>; <code>vendor\etc\init\trustonic.rc</code> and <code>vendor\etc\init\vendor.trustonic.tee@1.1-service.rc</code> with the file below. If there are any modifications to be made, do so according to the stock file.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">tee.rc</p>
 <details><summary>Click to open</summary>
 <p dir="auto">
</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre> 
 <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">init</span>
    <span class="pl-c">#create mountpoint for /mnt/vendor/persist partition</span>
    <span class="pl-en">mkdir</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span> <span class="pl-c1">0771</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span>

    <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">post</span>-<span class="pl-en">fs</span>
        <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>
        <span class="pl-en">chmod</span> <span class="pl-c1">0771</span> /<span class="pl-en">mnt</span>/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>

    <span class="pl-c"># We restorecon /mnt/vendor/persist to set SEPolicy label.</span>
    <span class="pl-en">restorecon</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>

    <span class="pl-c"># Create mcRegistry to store failure record</span>
    <span class="pl-en">mkdir</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">mcRegistry</span> <span class="pl-c1">0771</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span>
    <span class="pl-en">mkdir</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">paytrigger</span>
    <span class="pl-en">chown</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-sr">/mnt/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">paytrigger</span>
    <span class="pl-en">chmod</span> <span class="pl-c1">0777</span> /<span class="pl-en">mnt</span>/<span class="pl-en">vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">persist</span>/<span class="pl-en">paytrigger</span>

    <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-en">post</span>-<span class="pl-en">fs</span>-<span class="pl-en">data</span>
        <span class="pl-c"># Create /data/vendor/key_provisioning dir and get proper encryption policy installed</span>
        <span class="pl-c"># Key Installation</span>
        <span class="pl-en">mkdir</span> <span class="pl-sr">/data/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">key_provisioning</span> <span class="pl-c1">0771</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span>

    <span class="pl-c"># For META/FACTORY mode</span>
    <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-pds">property</span>:<span class="pl-en">ro</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">crypto</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">state</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-en">unencrypted</span>
        <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/proc/bootprof</span> <span class="pl-s">"MOBICORE: create /data/vendor/mcRegistry ++ (unencrypted)"</span>
        <span class="pl-en">mkdir</span> <span class="pl-sr">/data/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">mcRegistry</span> <span class="pl-c1">0775</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span>
        <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/proc/bootprof</span> <span class="pl-s">"MOBICORE: create /data/vendor/mcRegistry -- (unencrypted)"</span>

    <span class="pl-c"># Normal mode, FBE</span>
    <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-pds">property</span>:<span class="pl-en">ro</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">crypto</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">type</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-en">file</span> && <span class="pl-en">property</span>:<span class="pl-en">ro</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">crypto</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">state</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-en">encrypted</span>
        <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/proc/bootprof</span> <span class="pl-s">"MOBICORE: create /data/vendor/mcRegistry ++ (FBE encrypted)"</span>
        <span class="pl-en">mkdir</span> <span class="pl-sr">/data/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">mcRegistry</span> <span class="pl-c1">0775</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span>
        <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/proc/bootprof</span> <span class="pl-s">"MOBICORE: create /data/vendor/mcRegistry -- (FBE encrypted)"</span>

    <span class="pl-c"># Normal mode, FDE</span>
    <span class="pl-en">on</span> <span class="pl-pds">property</span>:<span class="pl-en">vold</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">decrypt</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-en">trigger_restart_framework</span>
        <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/proc/bootprof</span> <span class="pl-s">"MOBICORE: create /data/vendor/mcRegistry ++ (FDE encrypted)"</span>
        <span class="pl-en">mkdir</span> <span class="pl-sr">/data/vendor</span>/<span class="pl-en">mcRegistry</span> <span class="pl-c1">0775</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span> <span class="pl-en">system</span>
        <span class="pl-en">write</span> <span class="pl-sr">/proc/bootprof</span> <span class="pl-s">"MOBICORE: create /data/vendor/mcRegistry -- (FDE encrypted)"</span></pre></div>
 <p dir="auto"></p>
 </details>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Depending on the Android and kernel version, your device has a few more dependent binary files and need more services files. You can check the <code>vendor\etc\init\vendor.mediatek.hardware.paytrigger@1.0-service.rc</code>. Add the <a href="https://android.googlesource.com/platform/system/core/+/master/init/README.md#options" rel="nofollow">line below</a> so that Custom Recovery has permission over the process. This should happen for all *-service files.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre>     <span class="pl-en">seclabel</span> <span class="pl-pds">u</span>:<span class="pl-en">r</span>:<span class="pl-pds">recovery</span>:<span class="pl-en">s0</span></pre></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">trustonic.rc</p>

</p><div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre>

 </details>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Files come from the stock system.img file and must be located in:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>\system\etc\vintf</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>manifest.xml</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><code>\system\lib64</code>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>libTeeClient.so</code></li>
<li><code>vendor.trustonic.tee@1.0.so</code></li>
<li><code>vendor.trustonic.tee@1.1.so</code></li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Copy all folders and all files within <code>\vendor</code> to the device tree under <code>android\brand\devicename\recovery\root\vendor\</code> and all files within <code>\system</code> to the device tree under <code>android\brand\devicename\recovery\root\system\</code>. Remember that the files <code>init.recovery.mtNNNN.rc</code> and <code>init.recovery.trustonic.rc</code> must be in <code>android\brand\devicename\recovery\root</code>.</p>
<p dir="auto">To better understand the organization of the structure with encryption/decryption files, an example below for an MT6877 device:</p>
<details><summary>Click to open</summary>
<p dir="auto">
</p><p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://gist.github.com/assets/25868008/0b2981dc-9d6f-4f92-a638-06cb9bcb5f05"><img src="https://gist.github.com/assets/25868008/0b2981dc-9d6f-4f92-a638-06cb9bcb5f05" alt="ksnip_20240208-191107" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<p dir="auto"></p>
</details>
<p dir="auto">From my experience, there are <a href="https://source.android.com/docs/core/architecture/kernel/android-common#feature-and-launch-kernels" rel="nofollow">devices shipped</a> with Android 11 with kernel 4.19, devices shipped with Android 12-13 with kernel 4.14 and with kernel 5.1.xxx that have beanpod with fully functional decryption.</p>
</article>

<div class="highlight highlight-source-yaml" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-s">As you can see, all of this was not easy or quick to write.</span></pre></div>

<div class="highlight highlight-source-ruby" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-v">So</span> <span class="pl-k">if</span> <span class="pl-en">you</span>'<span class="pl-en">ve</span> <span class="pl-en">read</span> <span class="pl-en">everything</span> <span class="pl-en">up</span> <span class="pl-en">to</span> <span class="pl-en">this</span> <span class="pl-en">point</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-en">congratulations!</span> <span class="pl-en">Go</span> <span class="pl-en">ahead</span> <span class="pl-k">and</span> <span class="pl-en">enjoy</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en"></span></pre></div>
